# fmrireg to pyfmrireg Feature Parity Tracking

This document tracks the porting progress from the R package `fmrireg` to Python `pyfmrireg`.

## Overview

- **R Package**: fmrireg v0.1.0
- **Python Package**: pyfmrireg
- **Porting Strategy**: Functionally faithful while pythonic
- **Dependencies**: 
  - pyfmrihrf (port of fmrihrf)
  - pyfmridesign (port of fmridesign)
  - pyfmridataset (port of fmridataset) - TO BE PORTED SEPARATELY

## Core Classes and Functions

### Model Classes

| R Class/Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `fmri_model` | `FMRIModel` | ❌ Not Started | Main model class combining event and baseline models |
| `fmri_lm` | `FMRILM` | ❌ Not Started | Linear model fitting class |
| `fmri_rlm` | `FMRIRLM` | ❌ Not Started | Robust linear model |
| `fmri_latent_lm` | `FMRILatentLM` | ❌ Not Started | Latent variable model |
| `fmri_betas` | `FMRIBetas` | ❌ Not Started | Beta estimation results |

### Core Modeling Functions

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `create_fmri_model()` | `create_fmri_model()` | ❌ Not Started | High-level model constructor |
| `fmri_model()` | `fmri_model()` | ❌ Not Started | Low-level constructor |
| `fmri_lm()` | `fmri_lm()` | ❌ Not Started | Fit linear model |
| `fmri_rlm()` | `fmri_rlm()` | ❌ Not Started | Fit robust linear model |
| `fmri_latent_lm()` | `fmri_latent_lm()` | ❌ Not Started | Fit latent model |

### Design Matrix Functions

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `design_matrix()` | `design_matrix()` | ❌ Not Started | Extract design matrix |
| `design_plot()` | `design_plot()` | ❌ Not Started | Plot design matrix |
| `term_matrices()` | `term_matrices()` | ❌ Not Started | Extract term-wise matrices |
| `correlation_map()` | `correlation_map()` | ❌ Not Started | Design correlation heatmap |

### Contrast Functions

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `contrast()` | `contrast()` | ❌ Not Started | Define contrasts |
| `Fcontrasts()` | `f_contrasts()` | ❌ Not Started | F-contrasts |
| `fit_contrasts()` | `fit_contrasts()` | ❌ Not Started | Compute contrast statistics |
| `one_against_all_contrast()` | `one_against_all_contrast()` | ❌ Not Started | One vs all contrasts |
| `pair_contrast()` | `pair_contrast()` | ❌ Not Started | Pairwise contrasts |
| `pairwise_contrasts()` | `pairwise_contrasts()` | ❌ Not Started | All pairwise contrasts |
| `poly_contrast()` | `poly_contrast()` | ❌ Not Started | Polynomial contrasts |
| `unit_contrast()` | `unit_contrast()` | ❌ Not Started | Unit contrasts |

### Statistical Functions

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `estimate_betas()` | `estimate_betas()` | ❌ Not Started | Beta estimation |
| `standard_error()` | `standard_error()` | ❌ Not Started | Standard errors |
| `p_values()` | `p_values()` | ❌ Not Started | P-value computation |
| `stats()` | `stats()` | ❌ Not Started | Statistical summary |
| `estcon()` | `estcon()` | ❌ Not Started | Estimate contrasts |

### GLM Solver Functions

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `solve_glm_core()` | `solve_glm_core()` | ❌ Not Started | Core GLM solver |
| `chunkwise_lm()` | `chunkwise_lm()` | ❌ Not Started | Chunked processing |
| `runwise_lm()` | `runwise_lm()` | ❌ Not Started | Run-wise fitting |
| `multiresponse_lm()` | `multiresponse_lm()` | ❌ Not Started | Multi-response GLM |

### AR Modeling Functions

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `ar_parameters()` | `ar_parameters()` | ❌ Not Started | Estimate AR parameters |
| `ar_whiten_inplace()` | `ar_whiten_inplace()` | ❌ Not Started | C++ function to port |
| `ar_whiten_void()` | `ar_whiten_void()` | ❌ Not Started | C++ function to port |

### Bootstrap Functions

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `multiresponse_bootstrap_lm()` | `multiresponse_bootstrap_lm()` | ❌ Not Started | Bootstrap analysis |

### HRF Functions (from pyfmrihrf)

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `HRF()` | Use pyfmrihrf | 🔄 External | From pyfmrihrf package |
| `gen_hrf()` | Use pyfmrihrf | 🔄 External | From pyfmrihrf package |
| `hrf_spmg1()` | Use pyfmrihrf | 🔄 External | From pyfmrihrf package |
| `convolve()` | Use pyfmrihrf | 🔄 External | From pyfmrihrf package |

### Event/Design Functions (from pyfmridesign)

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `event_model()` | Use pyfmridesign | 🔄 External | From pyfmridesign package |
| `baseline_model()` | Use pyfmridesign | 🔄 External | From pyfmridesign package |
| `event_term()` | Use pyfmridesign | 🔄 External | From pyfmridesign package |

### Dataset Functions (from pyfmridataset - TO BE PORTED)

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `fmri_dataset()` | Use pyfmridataset | ⏳ Pending | Needs pyfmridataset package |
| `matrix_dataset()` | Use pyfmridataset | ⏳ Pending | Needs pyfmridataset package |
| `latent_dataset()` | Use pyfmridataset | ⏳ Pending | Needs pyfmridataset package |
| `fmri_mem_dataset()` | Use pyfmridataset | ⏳ Pending | Needs pyfmridataset package |

### Simulation Functions

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `simulate_bold_signal()` | `simulate_bold_signal()` | ❌ Not Started | BOLD simulation |
| `simulate_fmri_noise()` | `simulate_fmri_noise()` | ❌ Not Started | Noise simulation |
| `sim_ts()` | `sim_ts()` | ❌ Not Started | Time series simulation |

### AFNI Integration

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `afni_lm()` | `afni_lm()` | ❌ Not Started | AFNI GLM interface |
| `gen_afni_lm()` | `gen_afni_lm()` | ❌ Not Started | Generate AFNI commands |
| `build_afni_stims()` | `build_afni_stims()` | ❌ Not Started | AFNI stimulus files |
| `afni_hrf()` | `afni_hrf()` | ❌ Not Started | AFNI HRF models |

### Utility Functions

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `write_results()` | `write_results()` | ❌ Not Started | Save analysis results |
| `hrf_smoothing_kernel()` | `hrf_smoothing_kernel()` | ❌ Not Started | HRF-based smoothing |
| `shift()` | `shift()` | ❌ Not Started | Time shift operation |
| `soft_threshold()` | `soft_threshold()` | ❌ Not Started | Thresholding function |

### Visualization Functions

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `plot.fmri_model()` | `plot()` method | ❌ Not Started | Model visualization |
| `plot.event_model()` | Use pyfmridesign | 🔄 External | From pyfmridesign |
| `plot.regressor()` | Use pyfmridesign | 🔄 External | From pyfmridesign |
| `autoplot()` methods | `plot()` methods | ❌ Not Started | ggplot2-style plots |

## Test Files to Port

| Test File | Status | Notes |
|-----------|---------|--------|
| `test_fmrimodel.R` | ❌ Not Started | Core model tests |
| `test_fmriglm.R` | ❌ Not Started | GLM fitting tests |
| `test_ar_integration.R` | ❌ Not Started | AR modeling tests |
| `test_f_contrasts.R` | ❌ Not Started | F-statistic tests |
| `test_robust_glm_results.R` | ❌ Not Started | Robust regression tests |
| `test_dataset.R` | ⏳ Pending | Needs pyfmridataset |
| `test_bootstrap.R` | ❌ Not Started | Bootstrap tests |
| `test_contrasts.R` | ❌ Not Started | Contrast tests |
| `test_effective_df.R` | ❌ Not Started | Effective DF tests |
| `test_estimate_hrf.R` | ❌ Not Started | HRF estimation tests |
| `test_simulation.R` | ❌ Not Started | Simulation tests |
| `test_write_results.R` | ❌ Not Started | Output tests |
| ... (40+ more test files) | ❌ Not Started | See tests/testthat/ |

## Implementation Priority

1. **Phase 1**: Core infrastructure
   - Python package setup
   - Basic model classes (FMRIModel, FMRILM)
   - Test infrastructure

2. **Phase 2**: GLM functionality
   - GLM solvers
   - Basic contrast computation
   - Statistical functions

3. **Phase 3**: Advanced features
   - AR modeling
   - Robust regression
   - Bootstrap

4. **Phase 4**: Integration
   - AFNI interface
   - Result export
   - Visualization

5. **Phase 5**: Performance
   - Port C++ code
   - Optimize critical paths
   - Parallel processing

6. **Phase 6**: Dataset integration
   - Wait for pyfmridataset
   - Integrate dataset classes
   - Complete integration tests

## Status Legend

- ❌ Not Started
- 🚧 In Progress
- ✅ Completed
- 🔄 External (from other packages)
- ⏳ Pending (blocked on dependencies)

## Notes

- All functions should maintain numerical equivalence with R version
- Python API should be pythonic while preserving functionality
- Type hints should be added throughout
- Comprehensive docstrings following numpy style
- Performance-critical sections may use numba/cython

| `event_term()` | Use pyfmridesign | 🔄 External | From pyfmridesign package |

### Dataset Functions (from pyfmridataset - TO BE PORTED)

|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|

| `latent_dataset()` | Use pyfmridataset | ⏳ Pending | Needs pyfmridataset package |
| `fmri_mem_dataset()` | Use pyfmridataset | ⏳ Pending | Needs pyfmridataset package |

### Simulation Functions

| `simulate_fmri_noise()` | `simulate_fmri_noise()` | ❌ Not Started | Noise simulation |
| `sim_ts()` | `sim_ts()` | ❌ Not Started | Time series simulation |

### AFNI Integration

|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `afni_lm()` | `afni_lm()` | ❌ Not Started | AFNI GLM interface |
| `gen_afni_lm()` | `gen_afni_lm()` | ❌ Not Started | Generate AFNI commands |
| `build_afni_stims()` | `build_afni_stims()` | ❌ Not Started | AFNI stimulus files |
| `afni_hrf()` | `afni_hrf()` | ❌ Not Started | AFNI HRF models |

### Utility Functions

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `write_results()` | `write_results()` | ❌ Not Started | Save analysis results |
| `hrf_smoothing_kernel()` | `hrf_smoothing_kernel()` | ❌ Not Started | HRF-based smoothing |
| `shift()` | `shift()` | ❌ Not Started | Time shift operation |
| `soft_threshold()` | `soft_threshold()` | ❌ Not Started | Thresholding function |

### Visualization Functions

|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `plot.fmri_model()` | `plot()` method | ❌ Not Started | Model visualization |
| `plot.event_model()` | Use pyfmridesign | 🔄 External | From pyfmridesign |
| `plot.regressor()` | Use pyfmridesign | 🔄 External | From pyfmridesign |
| `autoplot()` methods | `plot()` methods | ❌ Not Started | ggplot2-style plots |

## Notes

- All functions should maintain numerical equivalence with R version

input.content:
# pyfmrireg Project Context

## Overview

This is a Python port of the R package `fmrireg` for fMRI data analysis. The goal is to achieve feature parity with the R version while maintaining pythonic code style.

## Project Structure

- **Source Package**: fmrireg (R) - located at ~/code/fmrireg
- **Target Package**: pyfmrireg (Python) - current directory
- **Related Packages**:
  - pyfmrihrf (~/code/pyfmrihrf) - Python port of fmrihrf
  - pyfmridesign (~/code/pyfmridesign) - Python port of fmridesign
  - pyfmridataset (TO BE PORTED) - Will be a separate package

## Porting Guidelines

Follow the R2Py guidance document (R2Py_guidance.md) for general porting principles.

### Key Principles

1. **Functional Faithfulness**: Maintain exact functionality and numerical equivalence
2. **Pythonic Style**: Use Python idioms and conventions
3. **Type Safety**: Add type hints throughout
4. **Modern Python**: Target Python 3.9+ with modern packaging (pyproject.toml)
5. **Testing**: Port all tests and ensure they pass

### Naming Conventions

- R functions in snake_case remain snake_case in Python
- R S3 classes become Python classes with PascalCase
- Generic functions become methods or standalone functions as appropriate

### Architecture Mapping

| R Pattern | Python Pattern |
|-----------|---------------|
| S3 classes | dataclasses or regular classes |
| S4 classes with validation | pydantic models |
| Generic functions | methods or functools.singledispatch |
| Vectorized operations | NumPy operations |
| data.frame | pandas DataFrame |
| Formulas | patsy or custom implementation |

### Dependencies

Core Python dependencies:
- numpy: Numerical operations
- scipy: Statistical functions
- pandas: Data structures
- matplotlib/seaborn: Visualization
- statsmodels: Statistical models
- pytest: Testing

## Progress Tracking

See `feature_parity.md` for detailed function-by-function porting status.

## Testing Strategy

1. **Golden Tests**: Compare outputs with R version
2. **Unit Tests**: Port all R tests to pytest
3. **Integration Tests**: End-to-end workflows
4. **Numerical Tolerance**: Use np.testing.assert_allclose with appropriate tolerances

## Performance Considerations

- Port C++ code (ar_whiten) to NumPy/Numba
- Use vectorized operations wherever possible
- Consider Dask for large-scale parallel processing

## Common Commands

```bash
# Run tests
pytest

# Run specific test file
pytest tests/test_fmri_model.py

# Check type hints
mypy pyfmrireg

# Format code
black pyfmrireg
```

## Important Files

- `feature_parity.md`: Tracks porting progress
- `R2Py_guidance.md`: General R to Python porting guidelines
- `tests/`: All test files (to be ported from R)
- `pyfmrireg/`: Main package code

## Notes for Future Development

- Dataset classes will come from pyfmridataset package (to be developed)
- AFNI integration may need special attention for Python environment
- Consider adding scikit-learn compatible API for some functions

[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools>=61.0", "wheel", "setuptools-scm>=6.2"]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"

[project]
name = "pyfmrireg"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Python package for fMRI regression analysis"
readme = "README.md"
requires-python = ">=3.9"
license = {text = "MIT"}
authors = [
    {name = "Your Name", email = "your.email@example.com"},
]
keywords = ["fmri", "neuroimaging", "statistics", "regression"]
classifiers = [
    "Development Status :: 3 - Alpha",
    "Intended Audience :: Science/Research",
    "License :: OSI Approved :: MIT License",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.9",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.10",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.11",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.12",
    "Topic :: Scientific/Engineering :: Medical Science Apps.",
    "Topic :: Scientific/Engineering :: Information Analysis",
]

dependencies = [
    "numpy>=1.21.0",
    "scipy>=1.7.0",
    "pandas>=1.3.0",
    "matplotlib>=3.4.0",
    "seaborn>=0.11.0",
    "statsmodels>=0.12.0",
    "scikit-learn>=1.0.0",
    "patsy>=0.5.0",
    "joblib>=1.0.0",
    "tqdm>=4.62.0",
    "pydantic>=2.0.0",
    "typing-extensions>=4.0.0",
]

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = [
    "pytest>=7.0.0",
    "pytest-cov>=3.0.0",
    "pytest-xdist>=2.5.0",
    "black>=22.0.0",
    "isort>=5.10.0",
    "flake8>=4.0.0",
    "mypy>=0.950",
    "pre-commit>=2.17.0",
    "ipython>=8.0.0",
    "jupyter>=1.0.0",
    "sphinx>=4.5.0",
    "sphinx-rtd-theme>=1.0.0",
    "numpydoc>=1.2.0",
]

performance = [
    "numba>=0.55.0",
    "dask[complete]>=2022.1.0",
    "ray>=1.10.0",
]

visualization = [
    "plotly>=5.6.0",
    "altair>=4.2.0",
    "bokeh>=2.4.0",
]

[project.urls]
Homepage = "https://github.com/yourusername/pyfmrireg"
Documentation = "https://pyfmrireg.readthedocs.io"
Repository = "https://github.com/yourusername/pyfmrireg"
Issues = "https://github.com/yourusername/pyfmrireg/issues"

[tool.setuptools]
packages = ["pyfmrireg"]

[tool.setuptools.package-data]
pyfmrireg = ["data/*.txt", "data/*.csv", "data/*.nii.gz"]

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
minversion = "7.0"
addopts = "-ra -q --strict-markers"
testpaths = ["tests"]
markers = [
    "slow: marks tests as slow (deselect with '-m \"not slow\"')",
    "integration: marks tests as integration tests",
    "requires_r: marks tests that require R to be installed",
]

[tool.black]
line-length = 88
target-version = ['py39']
include = '\.pyi?$'

[tool.isort]
profile = "black"
multi_line_output = 3
include_trailing_comma = true
force_grid_wrap = 0
use_parentheses = true
ensure_newline_before_comments = true
line_length = 88

[tool.mypy]
python_version = "3.9"
warn_return_any = true
warn_unused_configs = true
ignore_missing_imports = true
strict_optional = true
disallow_untyped_defs = true

[tool.coverage.run]
source = ["pyfmrireg"]
omit = ["*/tests/*", "*/test_*.py"]

[tool.coverage.report]
exclude_lines = [
    "pragma: no cover",
    "def __repr__",
    "if self.debug:",
    "if __name__ == .__main__.:",
    "raise AssertionError",
    "raise NotImplementedError",
]

input.content:
# pyfmrireg

Python package for fMRI regression analysis - a port of the R package `fmrireg`.

## Overview

`pyfmrireg` provides a comprehensive framework for analyzing functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) data, including:

- Flexible hemodynamic response function (HRF) modeling
- Event-related and block design support
- Multiple regression strategies (OLS, robust, AR-corrected)
- Contrast computation and statistical inference
- Integration with standard neuroimaging workflows

## Installation

```bash
pip install pyfmrireg
```

For development installation:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/yourusername/pyfmrireg.git
cd pyfmrireg
pip install -e ".[dev]"
```

## Quick Start

```python
import pyfmrireg as pfr
import numpy as np

# Create an fMRI model
model = pfr.create_fmri_model(
    dataset=my_fmri_data,
    event_model=my_events,
    hrf="spmg1",
    baseline="polynomial",
    degree=2
)

# Fit the model
results = pfr.fmri_lm(model)

# Compute contrasts
contrasts = pfr.contrast(results, "condition_A - condition_B")
stats = pfr.fit_contrasts(results, contrasts)

# View results
print(stats.summary())
```

## Features

- **Model Specification**: Flexible formula-based model specification
- **HRF Options**: Multiple built-in HRF models and custom HRF support
- **Regression Methods**: 
  - Ordinary least squares (OLS)
  - Robust regression
  - AR-corrected models
  - Latent variable models
- **Statistical Inference**: t-tests, F-tests, multiple comparison correction
- **Visualization**: Design matrix plots, statistical maps, diagnostic plots
- **Performance**: Optimized implementations with optional GPU acceleration

## Documentation

Full documentation is available at: https://pyfmrireg.readthedocs.io

## Dependencies

- Python >= 3.9
- NumPy >= 1.21
- SciPy >= 1.7
- pandas >= 1.3
- matplotlib >= 3.4
- statsmodels >= 0.12

## Related Packages

- `pyfmrihrf`: HRF modeling utilities
- `pyfmridesign`: Experimental design specification
- `pyfmridataset`: fMRI dataset handling (coming soon)

## Contributing

Contributions are welcome! Please see our [contributing guidelines](CONTRIBUTING.md).

## License

MIT License - see [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details.

## Citation

If you use this software, please cite:

```bibtex
@software{pyfmrireg,
  title = {pyfmrireg: Python package for fMRI regression analysis},
  author = {Your Name},
  year = {2024},
  url = {https://github.com/yourusername/pyfmrireg}
}
```

## Acknowledgments

This is a Python port of the R package `fmrireg`. We thank the original authors for their work.

- **Performance**: Optimized implementations with optional GPU acceleration

## Documentation

Full documentation is available at: https://pyfmrireg.readthedocs.io

## Dependencies

- Python >= 3.9
- NumPy >= 1.21
- SciPy >= 1.7
- pandas >= 1.3
- matplotlib >= 3.4
- statsmodels >= 0.12

## Related Packages

- `pyfmrihrf`: HRF modeling utilities
- `pyfmridesign`: Experimental design specification
- `pyfmridataset`: fMRI dataset handling (coming soon)

## Contributing

Contributions are welcome! Please see our [contributing guidelines](CONTRIBUTING.md).

## License

MIT License - see [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details.

## Citation

If you use this software, please cite:

```bibtex
@software{pyfmrireg,
  title = {pyfmrireg: Python package for fMRI regression analysis},
  author = {Your Name},
  year = {2024},
  url = {https://github.com/yourusername/pyfmrireg}
}
```

## Acknowledgments

This is a Python port of the R package `fmrireg`. We thank the original authors for their work.

"""
pyfmrireg - Python package for fMRI regression analysis.

A comprehensive framework for analyzing fMRI data with flexible modeling options.
"""

__version__ = "0.1.0"

# Core model functions
from .models.fmri_model import (
    FMRIModel,
    create_fmri_model,
    fmri_model,
)

from .models.fmri_lm import (
    FMRILM,
    fmri_lm,
)

from .models.fmri_rlm import (
    FMRIRLM,
    fmri_rlm,
)

from .models.fmri_latent_lm import (
    FMRILatentLM,
    fmri_latent_lm,
)

# Design matrix functions
from .models.design import (
    design_matrix,
    design_plot,
    term_matrices,
    correlation_map,
)

# Contrast functions
from .contrasts.contrast import (
    contrast,
    f_contrasts,
    fit_contrasts,
    one_against_all_contrast,
    pair_contrast,
    pairwise_contrasts,
    poly_contrast,
    unit_contrast,
)

# Statistical functions

    estimate_betas,
    standard_error,
    p_values,
    stats,
    estcon,
)

    solve_glm_core,
    chunkwise_lm,
    runwise_lm,
    multiresponse_lm,
)

    ar_parameters,
    ar_whiten_inplace,
)

)

content.text: Let's create a setup.cfg file for additional configuration:

input.content:
[metadata]
name = pyfmrireg
version = attr: pyfmrireg.__version__

[options]
packages = find:
python_requires = >=3.9
include_package_data = True

[options.packages.find]
where = .
include = pyfmrireg*
exclude = tests*

[options.package_data]
pyfmrireg = 
    data/*.txt
    data/*.csv
    data/*.json
    data/*.nii.gz

[flake8]
max-line-length = 88
extend-ignore = E203, W503
exclude = 
    .git,
    __pycache__,
    build,
    dist,
    .eggs,
    *.egg-info,
    .mypy_cache,
    .pytest_cache,
    .tox,
    venv

[mypy]
python_version = 3.9
warn_return_any = True
warn_unused_configs = True
ignore_missing_imports = True

[mypy-tests.*]
ignore_errors = True

[tool:pytest]
testpaths = tests
python_files = test_*.py
python_classes = Test*
python_functions = test_*
addopts = 
    -v
    --strict-markers
    --tb=short
    --cov=pyfmrireg
    --cov-report=term-missing:skip-covered
    --cov-report=html
    --cov-report=xml

[coverage:run]
source = pyfmrireg
omit = 
    */tests/*
    */test_*.py
    */__init__.py

[coverage:report]
exclude_lines =
    pragma: no cover
    def __repr__
    def __str__
    raise AssertionError
    raise NotImplementedError
    if __name__ == .__main__.:
    if TYPE_CHECKING:
    @abstract

content.text: Now let's create a GitHub Actions workflow for CI:

input.content:
name: Tests

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, develop ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
        python-version: ['3.9', '3.10', '3.11', '3.12']
        exclude:
          # Exclude some combinations to save CI time
          - os: macos-latest
            python-version: '3.9'
          - os: windows-latest
            python-version: '3.9'

    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v3
    
    - name: Set up Python ${{ matrix.python-version }}
      uses: actions/setup-python@v4
      with:
        python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
    
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: |
        python -m pip install --upgrade pip
        pip install -e ".[dev]"
    
    - name: Lint with flake8
      run: |
        flake8 pyfmrireg tests
    
    - name: Type check with mypy
      run: |
        mypy pyfmrireg
    
    - name: Test with pytest
      run: |
        pytest tests/ -v --cov=pyfmrireg --cov-report=xml
    
    - name: Upload coverage to Codecov
      uses: codecov/codecov-action@v3
      with:
        file: ./coverage.xml
        flags: unittests
        name: codecov-umbrella

  test-r-compatibility:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v3
    
    - name: Set up Python
      uses: actions/setup-python@v4
      with:
        python-version: '3.11'
    
    - name: Set up R
      uses: r-lib/actions/setup-r@v2
      with:
        r-version: '4.3'
    
    - name: Install R dependencies
      run: |
        Rscript -e "install.packages('remotes')"
        Rscript -e "remotes::install_github('bbuchsbaum/fmrireg')"
    
    - name: Install Python package
      run: |
        python -m pip install --upgrade pip
        pip install -e ".[dev]"
    
    - name: Run compatibility tests
      run: |
        pytest tests/integration/test_r_compatibility.py -v -m requires_r

I've successfully created the initial Python package structure for pyfmrireg. Here's what I've set up:

## Package Structure Created:

1. **Project Configuration**:
   - `pyproject.toml` - Modern Python packaging with dependencies
   - `setup.cfg` - Additional configuration for tools
   - `README.md` - Project documentation
   - `LICENSE` - MIT license
   - `.gitignore` - Git ignore patterns
   - `MANIFEST.in` - Package data inclusion

2. **Package Organization** (`pyfmrireg/`):
   - `models/` - Core model classes (FMRIModel, FMRILM, etc.)
   - `solvers/` - GLM solvers and fitting strategies
   - `stats/` - Statistical inference functions
   - `contrasts/` - Contrast computation
   - `utils/` - Utility functions
   - `visualization/` - Plotting functions
   - `io/` - Input/output and AFNI integration
   - `ar/` - Autoregressive modeling
   - `robust/` - Robust regression
   - `bootstrap/` - Bootstrap methods
   - `simulation/` - Simulation utilities

3. **Test Infrastructure**:
   - `tests/` - Main test directory
   - `tests/conftest.py` - Pytest fixtures and configuration
   - `tests/unit/` - Unit tests
   - `tests/integration/` - Integration tests
   - `tests/fixtures/` - Test data

4. **CI/CD**:
   - `.github/workflows/tests.yml` - GitHub Actions for testing
   - Includes Python/R compatibility testing

5. **Main Package File**:
   - `pyfmrireg/__init__.py` - Package imports matching R exports

The structure is ready for implementation. Next steps would be to start porting the core model classes (FMRIModel and FMRILM).

You're absolutely right to question that. ALL CAPS for class names is not pythonic. According to PEP 8, class names should use CapWords (PascalCase) convention.

"""
pyfmrireg - Python package for fMRI regression analysis.

A comprehensive framework for analyzing fMRI data with flexible modeling options.
"""

__version__ = "0.1.0"

    FMRIModel,
    create_fmri_model,
    fmri_model,
)

    FMRILM,
    fmri_lm,
)

    fmri_rlm,

)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# fmrireg to pyfmrireg Feature Parity Tracking

This document tracks the porting progress from the R package `fmrireg` to Python `pyfmrireg`.

## Overview

- **R Package**: fmrireg v0.1.0
- **Python Package**: pyfmrireg
- **Porting Strategy**: Functionally faithful while pythonic
- **Dependencies**: 
  - pyfmrihrf (port of fmrihrf)
  - pyfmridesign (port of fmridesign)
  - pyfmridataset (port of fmridataset) - TO BE PORTED SEPARATELY

## Core Classes and Functions

### Model Classes

| R Class/Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `fmri_model` | `FMRIModel` | ❌ Not Started | Main model class combining event and baseline models |
| `fmri_lm` | `FMRILM` | ❌ Not Started | Linear model fitting class |
| `fmri_rlm` | `FMRIRLM` | ❌ Not Started | Robust linear model |
| `fmri_latent_lm` | `FMRILatentLM` | ❌ Not Started | Latent variable model |
| `fmri_betas` | `FMRIBetas` | ❌ Not Started | Beta estimation results |

### Core Modeling Functions

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `create_fmri_model()` | `create_fmri_model()` | ❌ Not Started | High-level model constructor |
| `fmri_model()` | `fmri_model()` | ❌ Not Started | Low-level constructor |
| `fmri_lm()` | `fmri_lm()` | ❌ Not Started | Fit linear model |
| `fmri_rlm()` | `fmri_rlm()` | ❌ Not Started | Fit robust linear model |
| `fmri_latent_lm()` | `fmri_latent_lm()` | ❌ Not Started | Fit latent model |

### Design Matrix Functions

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `design_matrix()` | `design_matrix()` | ❌ Not Started | Extract design matrix |
| `design_plot()` | `design_plot()` | ❌ Not Started | Plot design matrix |
| `term_matrices()` | `term_matrices()` | ❌ Not Started | Extract term-wise matrices |
| `correlation_map()` | `correlation_map()` | ❌ Not Started | Design correlation heatmap |

### Contrast Functions

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `contrast()` | `contrast()` | ❌ Not Started | Define contrasts |
| `Fcontrasts()` | `f_contrasts()` | ❌ Not Started | F-contrasts |
| `fit_contrasts()` | `fit_contrasts()` | ❌ Not Started | Compute contrast statistics |
| `one_against_all_contrast()` | `one_against_all_contrast()` | ❌ Not Started | One vs all contrasts |
| `pair_contrast()` | `pair_contrast()` | ❌ Not Started | Pairwise contrasts |
| `pairwise_contrasts()` | `pairwise_contrasts()` | ❌ Not Started | All pairwise contrasts |
| `poly_contrast()` | `poly_contrast()` | ❌ Not Started | Polynomial contrasts |
| `unit_contrast()` | `unit_contrast()` | ❌ Not Started | Unit contrasts |

### Statistical Functions

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `estimate_betas()` | `estimate_betas()` | ❌ Not Started | Beta estimation |
| `standard_error()` | `standard_error()` | ❌ Not Started | Standard errors |
| `p_values()` | `p_values()` | ❌ Not Started | P-value computation |
| `stats()` | `stats()` | ❌ Not Started | Statistical summary |
| `estcon()` | `estcon()` | ❌ Not Started | Estimate contrasts |

### GLM Solver Functions

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `solve_glm_core()` | `solve_glm_core()` | ❌ Not Started | Core GLM solver |
| `chunkwise_lm()` | `chunkwise_lm()` | ❌ Not Started | Chunked processing |
| `runwise_lm()` | `runwise_lm()` | ❌ Not Started | Run-wise fitting |
| `multiresponse_lm()` | `multiresponse_lm()` | ❌ Not Started | Multi-response GLM |

### AR Modeling Functions

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `ar_parameters()` | `ar_parameters()` | ❌ Not Started | Estimate AR parameters |
| `ar_whiten_inplace()` | `ar_whiten_inplace()` | ❌ Not Started | C++ function to port |
| `ar_whiten_void()` | `ar_whiten_void()` | ❌ Not Started | C++ function to port |

### Bootstrap Functions

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `multiresponse_bootstrap_lm()` | `multiresponse_bootstrap_lm()` | ❌ Not Started | Bootstrap analysis |

### HRF Functions (from pyfmrihrf)

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `HRF()` | Use pyfmrihrf | 🔄 External | From pyfmrihrf package |
| `gen_hrf()` | Use pyfmrihrf | 🔄 External | From pyfmrihrf package |
| `hrf_spmg1()` | Use pyfmrihrf | 🔄 External | From pyfmrihrf package |
| `convolve()` | Use pyfmrihrf | 🔄 External | From pyfmrihrf package |

### Event/Design Functions (from pyfmridesign)

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `event_model()` | Use pyfmridesign | 🔄 External | From pyfmridesign package |
| `baseline_model()` | Use pyfmridesign | 🔄 External | From pyfmridesign package |
| `event_term()` | Use pyfmridesign | 🔄 External | From pyfmridesign package |

### Dataset Functions (from pyfmridataset - TO BE PORTED)

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `fmri_dataset()` | Use pyfmridataset | ⏳ Pending | Needs pyfmridataset package |
| `matrix_dataset()` | Use pyfmridataset | ⏳ Pending | Needs pyfmridataset package |
| `latent_dataset()` | Use pyfmridataset | ⏳ Pending | Needs pyfmridataset package |
| `fmri_mem_dataset()` | Use pyfmridataset | ⏳ Pending | Needs pyfmridataset package |

### Simulation Functions

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `simulate_bold_signal()` | `simulate_bold_signal()` | ❌ Not Started | BOLD simulation |
| `simulate_fmri_noise()` | `simulate_fmri_noise()` | ❌ Not Started | Noise simulation |
| `sim_ts()` | `sim_ts()` | ❌ Not Started | Time series simulation |

### AFNI Integration

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `afni_lm()` | `afni_lm()` | ❌ Not Started | AFNI GLM interface |
| `gen_afni_lm()` | `gen_afni_lm()` | ❌ Not Started | Generate AFNI commands |
| `build_afni_stims()` | `build_afni_stims()` | ❌ Not Started | AFNI stimulus files |
| `afni_hrf()` | `afni_hrf()` | ❌ Not Started | AFNI HRF models |

### Utility Functions

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `write_results()` | `write_results()` | ❌ Not Started | Save analysis results |
| `hrf_smoothing_kernel()` | `hrf_smoothing_kernel()` | ❌ Not Started | HRF-based smoothing |
| `shift()` | `shift()` | ❌ Not Started | Time shift operation |
| `soft_threshold()` | `soft_threshold()` | ❌ Not Started | Thresholding function |

### Visualization Functions

| R Function | Python Equivalent | Status | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| `plot.fmri_model()` | `plot()` method | ❌ Not Started | Model visualization |
| `plot.event_model()` | Use pyfmridesign | 🔄 External | From pyfmridesign |
| `plot.regressor()` | Use pyfmridesign | 🔄 External | From pyfmridesign |
| `autoplot()` methods | `plot()` methods | ❌ Not Started | ggplot2-style plots |

## Test Files to Port

| Test File | Status | Notes |
|-----------|---------|--------|
| `test_fmrimodel.R` | ❌ Not Started | Core model tests |
| `test_fmriglm.R` | ❌ Not Started | GLM fitting tests |
| `test_ar_integration.R` | ❌ Not Started | AR modeling tests |
| `test_f_contrasts.R` | ❌ Not Started | F-statistic tests |
| `test_robust_glm_results.R` | ❌ Not Started | Robust regression tests |
| `test_dataset.R` | ⏳ Pending | Needs pyfmridataset |
| `test_bootstrap.R` | ❌ Not Started | Bootstrap tests |
| `test_contrasts.R` | ❌ Not Started | Contrast tests |
| `test_effective_df.R` | ❌ Not Started | Effective DF tests |
| `test_estimate_hrf.R` | ❌ Not Started | HRF estimation tests |
| `test_simulation.R` | ❌ Not Started | Simulation tests |
| `test_write_results.R` | ❌ Not Started | Output tests |
| ... (40+ more test files) | ❌ Not Started | See tests/testthat/ |

## Implementation Priority

1. **Phase 1**: Core infrastructure
   - Python package setup
   - Basic model classes (FMRIModel, FMRILM)
   - Test infrastructure

2. **Phase 2**: GLM functionality
   - GLM solvers
   - Basic contrast computation
   - Statistical functions

3. **Phase 3**: Advanced features
   - AR modeling
   - Robust regression
   - Bootstrap

4. **Phase 4**: Integration
   - AFNI interface
   - Result export
   - Visualization

5. **Phase 5**: Performance
   - Port C++ code
   - Optimize critical paths
   - Parallel processing

6. **Phase 6**: Dataset integration
   - Wait for pyfmridataset
   - Integrate dataset classes
   - Complete integration tests

## Status Legend

- ❌ Not Started
- 🚧 In Progress
- ✅ Completed
- 🔄 External (from other packages)
- ⏳ Pending (blocked on dependencies)

content.text:
You're absolutely right! The pythonic convention is:
- `FmriModel` instead of `FMRIModel`
- `FmriLm` instead of `FMRILM`
- `FmriRlm` instead of `FMRIRLM`
- `FmriLatentLm` instead of `FMRILatentLM`

This follows PEP 8's recommendation to capitalize abbreviations as words, not all caps. So "Fmri" not "FMRI", and "Lm" not "LM". This is much more readable and pythonic.

###############################################################################
## fmrimodel.R
##
## This file creates the overall fMRI model by combining an event model 
## (describing experimental events) and a baseline model (modeling drift, 
## nuisance, and block effects).
##
## The file provides two main functions:
##   - create_fmri_model(): Creates an fMRI model from a formula, block formula,
##     (optionally) a baseline_model, and an fmri_dataset.
##   - fmri_model(): Combines an event_model and a baseline_model into an fmri_model.
##
## Additional functions build design matrices, compute contrasts, print, and plot
## the overall fMRI model.
###############################################################################

## ============================================================================
## Section 1: fMRI Model Construction Functions
## ============================================================================

#' Create an fMRI Model
#'
#' This function creates an fMRI model consisting of an event model and a baseline model.
#'
#' @param formula The model formula for experimental events.
#' @param block The model formula for block structure.
#' @param baseline_model (Optional) A \code{baseline_model} object. Default is \code{NULL}.
#' @param dataset An \code{fmri_dataset} object containing the time-series data.
#' @param drop_empty Logical. Whether to remove factor levels with zero size. Default is \code{TRUE}.
#' @param durations A vector of event durations. Default is \code{0}.
#' @return An \code{fmri_model} object.
#' @export
#' @examples
#' \dontrun{
#' # Assuming you have an fmri_dataset object named ds and a formula for events:
#' fmri_mod <- create_fmri_model(formula = onset ~ hrf(x) + hrf(y),
#'                               block = ~ run,
#'                               dataset = ds,
#'                               drop_empty = TRUE,
#'                               durations = rep(0, nrow(ds$event_table)))
#' }
create_fmri_model <- function(formula, block, baseline_model = NULL, dataset, drop_empty = TRUE, durations = 0) {
  assert_that(is.formula(formula), msg = "'formula' must be a formula")
  assert_that(is.formula(block), msg = "'block' must be a formula")
  assert_that(inherits(dataset, "fmri_dataset"), msg = "'dataset' must be an 'fmri_dataset'")
  assert_that(is.numeric(durations), msg = "'durations' must be numeric")
  
  # Replicate durations if a single value is provided.
  if (length(durations) == 1) {
    durations <- rep(durations, nrow(dataset$event_table))
  }
  
  # Resolve conflict: use a temporary variable to hold the baseline model.
  if (is.null(baseline_model)) {
    base_model_obj <- baseline_model(
      basis = "bs",
      degree = max(ceiling(median(fmrihrf::blocklens(dataset$sampling_frame)) / 100), 3),
      sframe = dataset$sampling_frame
    )
  } else {
    assert_that(inherits(baseline_model, "baseline_model"),
                msg = "'baseline_model' must have class 'baseline_model'")
    base_model_obj <- baseline_model
  }
  
  ev_model <- event_model(
    x = formula,
    block = block,
    data = dataset$event_table,
    sampling_frame = dataset$sampling_frame,
    drop_empty = drop_empty,
    durations = durations
  )
  
  fmri_model(ev_model, base_model_obj, dataset)
}


#' Construct an fMRI Regression Model
#'
#' This function constructs an fMRI regression model consisting of an event model
#' and a baseline model. The resulting model can be used for the analysis of fMRI data.
#'
#' @param event_model An object of class "event_model" representing the event-related part of the fMRI regression model.
#' @param baseline_model An object of class "baseline_model" representing the baseline-related part of the fMRI regression model.
#' @param dataset An \code{fmri_dataset} used to build the model.
#' @return An object of class \code{fmri_model} containing the event and baseline models along with the dataset.
#' @export
#' @seealso event_model, baseline_model
fmri_model <- function(event_model, baseline_model, dataset) {
  assert_that(inherits(event_model, "event_model"))
  assert_that(inherits(baseline_model, "baseline_model"))
  assert_that(inherits(dataset, "fmri_dataset"))

  fmodel <- list(event_model = event_model,
                 baseline_model = baseline_model,
                 dataset = dataset)
  class(fmodel) <- "fmri_model"
  fmodel
}


#' (Internal) Prediction Matrix
#'
#' This function is intended to compute a prediction matrix for the model.
#' (Currently a stub.)
#'
#' @param x An fmri_model object.
#' @return (Not implemented)
#' @keywords internal
#' @noRd
prediction_matrix <- function(x) {
  stop("not implemented")
}


## ============================================================================
## Section 2: Design Matrix and Environment for fMRI Models
## ============================================================================

#' @method design_matrix fmri_model
#' @rdname design_matrix
#' @export
#' @importFrom tibble as_tibble
design_matrix.fmri_model <- function(x, blockid = NULL, ...) {
  suppressMessages(
    tibble::as_tibble(
      cbind(
        design_matrix(x$event_model, blockid),
        design_matrix(x$baseline_model, blockid)
      ),
      .name_repair = "check_unique"
    )
  )
}


#' @importFrom tibble as_tibble
#' @keywords internal
#' @noRd
design_env.fmri_model <- function(x, blockid = NULL) {
  stop("Not implemented")
}


## ============================================================================
## Section 3: Accessor Functions for fMRI Models
## ============================================================================

#' @export
terms.fmri_model <- function(x, ...) {
  c(terms(x$event_model), terms(x$baseline_model))
}

#' @export
#' @autoglobal
cells.fmri_model <- function(x, ...) {
  c1 <- cells(x$event_model) %>% dplyr::mutate(type = "event")
  c2 <- cells(x$baseline_model) %>% dplyr::mutate(type = "baseline")
  rbind(c1, c2) %>% dplyr::relocate(index, type)
}

#' @export
blocklens.fmri_model <- function(x, ...) {
  fmrihrf::blocklens(x$event_model)
}

#' @export
event_terms.fmri_model <- function(x) {
  terms(x$event_model)
}

#' @export
baseline_terms.fmri_model <- function(x) {
  terms(x$baseline_model)
}

#' @export
contrast_weights.fmri_model <- function(x, ...) {
  contrast_weights(x$event_model, ...)
}

#' @export
conditions.fmri_model <- function(x, ...) {
  unlist(lapply(terms(x), function(t) conditions(t)), use.names = FALSE)
}

#' @export
conditions.baseline_model <- function(x, ...) {
  unlist(lapply(terms(x), function(t) conditions(t)), use.names = FALSE)
}


## ============================================================================
## Section 4: Plot and Print Methods for fMRI Models
## ============================================================================

#' @export
plot.fmri_model <- function(x, ...) {
  with_package("cowplot")

#' @export
#' @rdname print
print.fmri_model <- function(x, ...) {
  # Header with fancy border
  cat("\n=============================================")
  cat("\n             fMRI Model                     ")
  cat("\n=============================================")
  
  # Event Model Section
  cat("\n Event Model                                ")
  cat("\n---------------------------------------------")
  cat("\n║ Formula:", crayon::cyan(Reduce(paste, deparse(x$event_model$model_spec$formula))))
  
  # Event Model Summary
  cat("\n║ Summary:")
  cat("\n║   • Terms:", crayon::yellow(length(terms(x$event_model))))
  cat("\n║   • Events:", crayon::yellow(nrow(x$event_model$model_spec$event_table)))
  cat("\n║   • Design Columns:", crayon::yellow(length(conditions(x$event_model))))
  cat("\n║   • Blocks:", crayon::yellow(length(unique(x$event_model$blockids))))
  
  # Baseline Model Section (if present)
  if (!is.null(x$baseline_model)) {
    cat("\n---------------------------------------------")
    cat("\n║ Baseline Model                           ║")
    cat("\n║ Components:")
    
    # Drift term info
    if (!is.null(x$baseline_model$drift_term)) {
      drift_name <- x$baseline_model$drift_term$varname
      basis_type <- x$baseline_model$drift_spec$basis
      degree <- x$baseline_model$drift_spec$degree
      drift_cols <- ncol(design_matrix(x$baseline_model$drift_term))
      cat("\n║   • Drift:", crayon::magenta(drift_name))
      cat("\n║     - Type:", crayon::blue(basis_type))
      cat("\n║     - Degree:", crayon::blue(degree))
      cat("\n║     - Columns:", crayon::yellow(drift_cols))
    }
    
    # Block term info
    if (!is.null(x$baseline_model$block_term)) {
      const_cols <- ncol(design_matrix(x$baseline_model$block_term))
      cat("\n║   • Block Terms:", crayon::yellow(const_cols), "columns")
    }
    
    # Nuisance term info
    if (!is.null(x$baseline_model$nuisance_term)) {
      nuis_cols <- ncol(design_matrix(x$baseline_model$nuisance_term))
      cat("\n║   • Nuisance Terms:", crayon::yellow(nuis_cols), "columns")
    }
  }
  
  # Total Model Summary
  cat("\n---------------------------------------------")
  cat("\n║ Total Model                              ║")
  total_cols <- ncol(design_matrix(x))
  cat("\n║   • Total Design Columns:", crayon::yellow(total_cols))
  
  # Footer
  cat("\n╚══════════════════════════════════════════╝\n")
}

#' correlation_map.fmri_model
#'
#' @description
#' Generates a correlation heatmap of the columns in an \code{fmri_model}'s combined
#' event+baseline design matrix.
#'
#' @param x An \code{fmri_model}.
#' @param method Correlation method (e.g., "pearson", "spearman").
#' @param half_matrix Logical; if TRUE, display only the lower triangle of the matrix.
#' @param absolute_limits Logical; if TRUE, set color scale limits from -1 to 1.
#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to internal plotting functions.
#' @export
correlation_map.fmri_model <- function(x,
                                       method          = c("pearson", "spearman"),
                                       half_matrix     = FALSE,
                                       absolute_limits = TRUE,
                                       ...) {
  DM <- as.matrix(design_matrix(x))
  .correlation_map_common(DM, method=method, half_matrix=half_matrix,
                          absolute_limits=absolute_limits, ...)
}

#' Optionally draws horizontal lines between blocks (runs), and rotates x‐axis
#' labels diagonally for readability.
#'
#' @param x An \code{fmri_model} object.

fMRI Model implementation.

This module provides the FmriModel class which combines event and baseline models
for fMRI regression analysis.
"""

from typing import Optional, Union, List, Dict, Any
from dataclasses import dataclass
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

# For now, we'll create placeholder imports - these will come from pyfmridesign

# Import our minimal dataset implementation

@dataclass
class FmriModel:
    """
    fMRI regression model combining event and baseline models.
    
    This class represents a complete fMRI model consisting of an event model
    (describing experimental events) and a baseline model (modeling drift,
    nuisance, and block effects).
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    event_model : EventModel
        Model for experimental events
    baseline_model : BaselineModel
        Model for baseline effects (drift, nuisance, etc.)
    dataset : Union[MatrixDataset, MultiRunMatrixDataset]
        The fMRI dataset
        
    Attributes
    ----------
    event_model : EventModel
        The event model component
    baseline_model : BaselineModel
        The baseline model component
    dataset : Union[MatrixDataset, MultiRunMatrixDataset]
        The associated dataset
    """
    event_model: EventModel
    baseline_model: BaselineModel
    dataset: Union[MatrixDataset, MultiRunMatrixDataset]
    
    def __post_init__(self):
        """Validate inputs."""
        # In the future, add validation when we have real EventModel/BaselineModel
        pass
    
    def design_matrix(self, block_id: Optional[int] = None) -> pd.DataFrame:
        """
        Get the combined design matrix.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        block_id : int, optional
            If provided, return design matrix for specific block/run
            
        Returns
        -------
        pd.DataFrame
            Combined design matrix with event and baseline regressors
        """
        # Placeholder implementation - will be completed when we have EventModel/BaselineModel
        # For now, return empty DataFrame
        return pd.DataFrame()
    
    def get_terms(self) -> List[str]:
        """
        Get all terms in the model.
        
        Returns
        -------
        list of str
            List of all term names
        """
        # Placeholder
        return []
    
    def get_event_terms(self) -> List[str]:

        Get event model terms.

        -------
        list of str
            List of event term names
        """
        # Placeholder
        return []
    
    def get_baseline_terms(self) -> List[str]:
        """
        Get baseline model terms.
        
        Returns
        -------
        list of str
            List of baseline term names
        """
        # Placeholder  
        return []
    
    def plot(self, figsize: tuple = (12, 8)) -> plt.Figure:
        """
        Plot the model design matrices.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        figsize : tuple
            Figure size (width, height)
            
        Returns
        -------
        plt.Figure
            Figure with event and baseline model plots
        """
        fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=figsize)
        
        # Placeholder visualization
        ax1.text(0.5, 0.5, 'Event Model', ha='center', va='center', transform=ax1.transAxes)
        ax1.set_title('Event Model')
        ax1.set_xticks([])
        ax1.set_yticks([])
        
        ax2.text(0.5, 0.5, 'Baseline Model', ha='center', va='center', transform=ax2.transAxes)
        ax2.set_title('Baseline Model') 
        ax2.set_xticks([])
        ax2.set_yticks([])
        
        plt.tight_layout()
        return fig
    
    def correlation_map(
        self,
        method: str = 'pearson',
        half_matrix: bool = False,
        absolute_limits: bool = True,
        figsize: tuple = (10, 8),
        **kwargs
    ) -> plt.Figure:
        """
        Generate correlation heatmap of design matrix columns.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        method : str
            Correlation method ('pearson' or 'spearman')
        half_matrix : bool
            If True, show only lower triangle
        absolute_limits : bool
            If True, set color scale limits to [-1, 1]
        figsize : tuple
            Figure size
        **kwargs
            Additional arguments for plotting
            
        Returns
        -------
        plt.Figure
            Correlation heatmap figure
        """
        # Get design matrix
        dm = self.design_matrix()
        
        if dm.empty:
            # Placeholder when design matrix not available
            fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=figsize)
            ax.text(0.5, 0.5, 'Design Matrix Not Available', 
                   ha='center', va='center', transform=ax.transAxes)
            return fig
        
        # Compute correlation
        if method == 'pearson':
            corr = dm.corr(method='pearson')
        elif method == 'spearman':
            corr = dm.corr(method='spearman')
        else:
            raise ValueError(f"Unknown correlation method: {method}")
        
        # Create mask for half matrix if requested
        mask = None
        if half_matrix:
            mask = np.triu(np.ones_like(corr, dtype=bool))
        
        # Set color limits
        vmin, vmax = (-1, 1) if absolute_limits else (None, None)
        
        # Create heatmap
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=figsize)
        sns.heatmap(
            corr,
            mask=mask,
            cmap='RdBu_r',
            center=0,
            vmin=vmin,
            vmax=vmax,
            square=True,
            ax=ax,
            cbar_kws={'label': f'{method.capitalize()} Correlation'},
            **kwargs
        )
        
        ax.set_title('Design Matrix Correlation Map')
        plt.tight_layout()
        
        return fig
    
    def __repr__(self):
        """String representation."""
        n_voxels = getattr(self.dataset, 'n_voxels', 'Unknown')
        return (
            f"FmriModel(\n"
            f"  event_model={self.event_model.__class__.__name__},\n"
            f"  baseline_model={self.baseline_model.__class__.__name__},\n"
            f"  dataset={self.dataset.__class__.__name__}(n_voxels={n_voxels})\n"
            f")"
        )


def create_fmri_model(
    formula: str,
    block: str,
    dataset: Union[MatrixDataset, MultiRunMatrixDataset],
    baseline_model: Optional[BaselineModel] = None,
    drop_empty: bool = True,
    durations: Union[float, np.ndarray] = 0.0,
    **kwargs
) -> FmriModel:
    """
    Create an fMRI model from formula specifications.
    
    This is a high-level constructor that creates an fMRI model from
    formula strings specifying the event and block structure.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    formula : str
        Model formula for experimental events (e.g., "onset ~ hrf(condition)")
    block : str  
        Model formula for block structure (e.g., "~ run")
    dataset : Union[MatrixDataset, MultiRunMatrixDataset]
        The fMRI dataset
    baseline_model : BaselineModel, optional
        Pre-specified baseline model. If None, creates default baseline
    drop_empty : bool
        Whether to remove factor levels with zero events
    durations : float or array-like
        Event durations in seconds
    **kwargs
        Additional arguments
        
    Returns
    -------
    FmriModel
        The constructed fMRI model
        
    Examples
    --------
    >>> dataset = create_dummy_dataset(n_timepoints=100, n_voxels=50)
    >>> model = create_fmri_model(
    ...     formula="onset ~ hrf(condition)",
    ...     block="~ run", 
    ...     dataset=dataset
    ... )
    """
    # This is a placeholder implementation
    # In the real implementation, we would:
    # 1. Parse the formula
    # 2. Create EventModel from formula and dataset
    # 3. Create BaselineModel if not provided
    # 4. Combine into FmriModel
    
    # For now, create placeholder models
    event_model = EventModel()
    
    if baseline_model is None:
        baseline_model = BaselineModel()
    
    return FmriModel(
        event_model=event_model,
        baseline_model=baseline_model,
        dataset=dataset
    )


def fmri_model(
    event_model: EventModel,
    baseline_model: BaselineModel,
    dataset: Union[MatrixDataset, MultiRunMatrixDataset]
) -> FmriModel:
    """
    Construct an fMRI regression model.
    
    This is the low-level constructor that combines pre-built event and
    baseline models with a dataset.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    event_model : EventModel
        Event model object
    baseline_model : BaselineModel
        Baseline model object  
    dataset : Union[MatrixDataset, MultiRunMatrixDataset]
        The fMRI dataset
        
    Returns
    -------
    FmriModel
        The constructed model
        
    Examples
    --------
    >>> # Assuming you have event_model and baseline_model objects:
    >>> dataset = create_dummy_dataset()
    >>> model = fmri_model(event_model, baseline_model, dataset)
    """
    return FmriModel(
        event_model=event_model,
        baseline_model=baseline_model,
        dataset=dataset
    )

"""
Tests for FmriModel class.
"""

import pytest
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from pyfmrireg.models.fmri_model import (
    FmriModel, 
    create_fmri_model,
    fmri_model,
    EventModel,
    BaselineModel
)
from pyfmrireg.data import MatrixDataset, create_dummy_dataset


class TestFmriModel:
    """Test FmriModel class."""
    
    def test_fmri_model_creation(self):
        """Test basic model creation."""
        # Create dummy components
        event_model = EventModel()
        baseline_model = BaselineModel()
        dataset = create_dummy_dataset(n_timepoints=100, n_voxels=10)
        
        # Create model
        model = FmriModel(
            event_model=event_model,
            baseline_model=baseline_model,
            dataset=dataset
        )
        
        assert isinstance(model, FmriModel)
        assert model.event_model is event_model
        assert model.baseline_model is baseline_model
        assert model.dataset is dataset
    
    def test_fmri_model_function(self):
        """Test fmri_model constructor function."""
        event_model = EventModel()
        baseline_model = BaselineModel()
        dataset = create_dummy_dataset(n_timepoints=100, n_voxels=10)
        
        model = fmri_model(event_model, baseline_model, dataset)
        
        assert isinstance(model, FmriModel)
        assert model.event_model is event_model
        assert model.baseline_model is baseline_model
        assert model.dataset is dataset
    
    def test_create_fmri_model_function(self):
        """Test high-level create_fmri_model function."""
        dataset = create_dummy_dataset(n_timepoints=100, n_voxels=10)
        
        model = create_fmri_model(
            formula="onset ~ hrf(condition)",
            block="~ run",
            dataset=dataset
        )
        
        assert isinstance(model, FmriModel)
        assert isinstance(model.event_model, EventModel)
        assert isinstance(model.baseline_model, BaselineModel)
        assert model.dataset is dataset
    
    def test_design_matrix_method(self):
        """Test design_matrix method."""
        event_model = EventModel()
        baseline_model = BaselineModel()
        dataset = create_dummy_dataset(n_timepoints=100, n_voxels=10)
        
        model = FmriModel(event_model, baseline_model, dataset)
        dm = model.design_matrix()
        
        assert isinstance(dm, pd.DataFrame)
        # Currently returns empty DataFrame in placeholder
    
    def test_get_terms_methods(self):
        """Test term accessor methods."""
        event_model = EventModel()
        baseline_model = BaselineModel()
        dataset = create_dummy_dataset(n_timepoints=100, n_voxels=10)
        
        model = FmriModel(event_model, baseline_model, dataset)
        
        all_terms = model.get_terms()
        event_terms = model.get_event_terms()
        baseline_terms = model.get_baseline_terms()
        
        assert isinstance(all_terms, list)
        assert isinstance(event_terms, list)
        assert isinstance(baseline_terms, list)
    
    def test_plot_method(self):
        """Test plot method."""
        event_model = EventModel()
        baseline_model = BaselineModel()
        dataset = create_dummy_dataset(n_timepoints=100, n_voxels=10)
        
        model = FmriModel(event_model, baseline_model, dataset)
        fig = model.plot()
        
        assert isinstance(fig, plt.Figure)
        assert len(fig.axes) == 2  # Should have 2 subplots
        plt.close(fig)
    
    def test_correlation_map_method(self):
        """Test correlation_map method."""
        event_model = EventModel()
        baseline_model = BaselineModel()
        dataset = create_dummy_dataset(n_timepoints=100, n_voxels=10)
        
        model = FmriModel(event_model, baseline_model, dataset)
        fig = model.correlation_map()
        
        assert isinstance(fig, plt.Figure)
        plt.close(fig)
    
    def test_repr(self):
        """Test string representation."""
        event_model = EventModel()
        baseline_model = BaselineModel()
        dataset = create_dummy_dataset(n_timepoints=100, n_voxels=10)
        
        model = FmriModel(event_model, baseline_model, dataset)
        repr_str = repr(model)
        
        assert "FmriModel" in repr_str
        assert "EventModel" in repr_str
        assert "BaselineModel" in repr_str
        assert "MatrixDataset" in repr_str
    
    def test_multirun_dataset(self):
        """Test with multi-run dataset."""
        event_model = EventModel()
        baseline_model = BaselineModel()
        dataset = create_dummy_dataset(
            n_timepoints=100, 
            n_voxels=10,
            n_runs=3
        )
        
        model = FmriModel(event_model, baseline_model, dataset)
        
        assert isinstance(model, FmriModel)
        assert hasattr(model.dataset, 'n_runs')
        assert model.dataset.n_runs == 3


class TestFmriModelIntegration:
    """Integration tests for FmriModel."""
    
    @pytest.mark.integration
    def test_model_with_real_data_structure(self):
        """Test model with more realistic data structure."""
        # Create dataset with specific properties
        np.random.seed(42)
        dataset = create_dummy_dataset(
            n_timepoints=200,
            n_voxels=100,
            n_runs=2,
            tr=2.0
        )
        
        # Create model
        model = create_fmri_model(
            formula="onset ~ hrf(face) + hrf(house)",
            block="~ run",
            dataset=dataset,
            drop_empty=True,
            durations=1.0
        )
        
        assert isinstance(model, FmriModel)
        assert model.dataset.n_voxels == 100
        
    def test_correlation_map_with_options(self):
        """Test correlation map with various options."""
        event_model = EventModel()
        baseline_model = BaselineModel()
        dataset = create_dummy_dataset(n_timepoints=100, n_voxels=10)
        
        model = FmriModel(event_model, baseline_model, dataset)
        
        # Test different correlation methods
        for method in ['pearson', 'spearman']:
            fig = model.correlation_map(method=method)
            assert isinstance(fig, plt.Figure)
            plt.close(fig)
        
        # Test half matrix
        fig = model.correlation_map(half_matrix=True)
        assert isinstance(fig, plt.Figure)
        plt.close(fig)
        
        # Test absolute limits
        fig = model.correlation_map(absolute_limits=False)
        assert isinstance(fig, plt.Figure)
        plt.close(fig)

"""
Tests for FmriModel class.
"""

import pytest
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from pyfmrireg.models.fmri_model import (
    FmriModel, 
    create_fmri_model,
    fmri_model,
    EventModel,
    BaselineModel
)
from pyfmrireg.data import MatrixDataset, create_dummy_dataset


class TestFmriModel:
    """Test FmriModel class."""
    
    def test_fmri_model_creation(self):
        """Test basic model creation."""
        # Create dummy components
        event_model = EventModel()
        baseline_model = BaselineModel()
        dataset = create_dummy_dataset(n_timepoints=100, n_voxels=10)
        
        # Create model
        model = FmriModel(
            event_model=event_model,
            baseline_model=baseline_model,
            dataset=dataset
        )
        
        assert isinstance(model, FmriModel)
        assert model.event_model is event_model
        assert model.baseline_model is baseline_model
        assert model.dataset is dataset
    
    def test_fmri_model_function(self):
        """Test fmri_model constructor function."""
        event_model = EventModel()
        baseline_model = BaselineModel()
        dataset = create_dummy_dataset(n_timepoints=100, n_voxels=10)
        
        model = fmri_model(event_model, baseline_model, dataset)
        
        assert isinstance(model, FmriModel)
        assert model.event_model is event_model
        assert model.baseline_model is baseline_model
        assert model.dataset is dataset
    
    def test_create_fmri_model_function(self):
        """Test high-level create_fmri_model function."""
        dataset = create_dummy_dataset(n_timepoints=100, n_voxels=10)
        
        model = create_fmri_model(
            formula="onset ~ hrf(condition)",
            block="~ run",
            dataset=dataset
        )
        
        assert isinstance(model, FmriModel)
        assert isinstance(model.event_model, EventModel)
        assert isinstance(model.baseline_model, BaselineModel)
        assert model.dataset is dataset
    
    def test_design_matrix_method(self):
        """Test design_matrix method."""
        event_model = EventModel()
        baseline_model = BaselineModel()
        dataset = create_dummy_dataset(n_timepoints=100, n_voxels=10)
        
        model = FmriModel(event_model, baseline_model, dataset)
        dm = model.design_matrix()
        
        assert isinstance(dm, pd.DataFrame)
        # Currently returns empty DataFrame in placeholder
    
    def test_get_terms_methods(self):
        """Test term accessor methods."""
        event_model = EventModel()
        baseline_model = BaselineModel()
        dataset = create_dummy_dataset(n_timepoints=100, n_voxels=10)
        
        model = FmriModel(event_model, baseline_model, dataset)
        
        all_terms = model.get_terms()
        event_terms = model.get_event_terms()
        baseline_terms = model.get_baseline_terms()
        
        assert isinstance(all_terms, list)
        assert isinstance(event_terms, list)
        assert isinstance(baseline_terms, list)
    
    def test_plot_method(self):
        """Test plot method."""
        event_model = EventModel()
        baseline_model = BaselineModel()
        dataset = create_dummy_dataset(n_timepoints=100, n_voxels=10)
        
        model = FmriModel(event_model, baseline_model, dataset)
        fig = model.plot()
        
        assert isinstance(fig, plt.Figure)
        assert len(fig.axes) == 2  # Should have 2 subplots
        plt.close(fig)
    
    def test_correlation_map_method(self):
        """Test correlation_map method."""
        event_model = EventModel()
        baseline_model = BaselineModel()
        dataset = create_dummy_dataset(n_timepoints=100, n_voxels=10)
        
        model = FmriModel(event_model, baseline_model, dataset)
        fig = model.correlation_map()
        
        assert isinstance(fig, plt.Figure)
        plt.close(fig)
    
    def test_repr(self):
        """Test string representation."""
        event_model = EventModel()
        baseline_model = BaselineModel()
        dataset = create_dummy_dataset(n_timepoints=100, n_voxels=10)
        
        model = FmriModel(event_model, baseline_model, dataset)
        repr_str = repr(model)
        
        assert "FmriModel" in repr_str
        assert "EventModel" in repr_str
        assert "BaselineModel" in repr_str
        assert "MatrixDataset" in repr_str
    
    def test_multirun_dataset(self):
        """Test with multi-run dataset."""
        event_model = EventModel()
        baseline_model = BaselineModel()
        dataset = create_dummy_dataset(
            n_timepoints=100, 
            n_voxels=10,
            n_runs=3
        )
        
        model = FmriModel(event_model, baseline_model, dataset)
        
        assert isinstance(model, FmriModel)
        assert hasattr(model.dataset, 'n_runs')
        assert model.dataset.n_runs == 3


class TestFmriModelIntegration:
    """Integration tests for FmriModel."""
    
    @pytest.mark.integration
    def test_model_with_real_data_structure(self):
        """Test model with more realistic data structure."""
        # Create dataset with specific properties
        np.random.seed(42)
        dataset = create_dummy_dataset(
            n_timepoints=200,
            n_voxels=100,
            n_runs=2,
            tr=2.0
        )
        
        # Create model
        model = create_fmri_model(
            formula="onset ~ hrf(face) + hrf(house)",
            block="~ run",
            dataset=dataset,
            drop_empty=True,
            durations=1.0
        )
        
        assert isinstance(model, FmriModel)
        assert model.dataset.n_voxels == 100
        
    def test_correlation_map_with_options(self):
        """Test correlation map with various options."""
        event_model = EventModel()
        baseline_model = BaselineModel()
        dataset = create_dummy_dataset(n_timepoints=100, n_voxels=10)
        
        model = FmriModel(event_model, baseline_model, dataset)
        
        # Test different correlation methods
        for method in ['pearson', 'spearman']:
            fig = model.correlation_map(method=method)
            assert isinstance(fig, plt.Figure)
            plt.close(fig)
        
        # Test half matrix
        fig = model.correlation_map(half_matrix=True)
        assert isinstance(fig, plt.Figure)
        plt.close(fig)
        
        # Test absolute limits
        fig = model.correlation_map(absolute_limits=False)
        assert isinstance(fig, plt.Figure)
        plt.close(fig)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""
pyfmrireg - Python package for fMRI regression analysis.

A comprehensive framework for analyzing fMRI data with flexible modeling options.
"""

__version__ = "0.1.0"

# Core model functions
from .models.fmri_model import (
    FmriModel,
    create_fmri_model,
    fmri_model,
)

from .models.fmri_lm import (
    FmriLm,
    fmri_lm,
)

from .models.fmri_rlm import (
    FmriRlm,
    fmri_rlm,
)

from .models.fmri_latent_lm import (
    FmriLatentLm,
    fmri_latent_lm,
)

# Design matrix functions
from .models.design import (
    design_matrix,
    design_plot,
    term_matrices,
    correlation_map,
)

# Contrast functions
from .contrasts.contrast import (
    contrast,
    f_contrasts,
    fit_contrasts,
    one_against_all_contrast,
    pair_contrast,
    pairwise_contrasts,
    poly_contrast,
    unit_contrast,
)

# Statistical functions
from .stats.inference import (
    estimate_betas,
    standard_error,
    p_values,
    stats,
    estcon,
)

# Solver functions
from .solvers.glm_solver import (
    solve_glm_core,
    chunkwise_lm,
    runwise_lm,
    multiresponse_lm,
)

# AR modeling
from .ar.ar_model import (
    ar_parameters,
    ar_whiten_inplace,
)

# Bootstrap
from .bootstrap.bootstrap import (
    multiresponse_bootstrap_lm,
)

# Simulation
from .simulation.simulate import (
    simulate_bold_signal,
    simulate_fmri_noise,
    sim_ts,
)

# Utilities
from .utils.utilities import (
    hrf_smoothing_kernel,
    shift,
    soft_threshold,
)

# I/O functions
from .io.results import (
    write_results,
)

# AFNI integration
from .io.afni import (
    afni_lm,
    gen_afni_lm,
    build_afni_stims,
    afni_hrf,
)

__all__ = [
    # Version
    "__version__",
    
    # Core models
    "FmriModel",
    "create_fmri_model",
    "fmri_model",
    "FmriLm",
    "fmri_lm",
    "FmriRlm", 
    "fmri_rlm",
    "FmriLatentLm",
    "fmri_latent_lm",
    
    # Design matrix
    "design_matrix",
    "design_plot",
    "term_matrices",
    "correlation_map",
    
    # Contrasts
    "contrast",
    "f_contrasts",
    "fit_contrasts",
    "one_against_all_contrast",
    "pair_contrast",
    "pairwise_contrasts",
    "poly_contrast",
    "unit_contrast",
    
    # Statistics
    "estimate_betas",
    "standard_error",
    "p_values",
    "stats",
    "estcon",
    
    # Solvers
    "solve_glm_core",
    "chunkwise_lm",
    "runwise_lm",
    "multiresponse_lm",
    
    # AR modeling
    "ar_parameters",
    "ar_whiten_inplace",
    
    # Bootstrap
    "multiresponse_bootstrap_lm",
    
    # Simulation
    "simulate_bold_signal",
    "simulate_fmri_noise",
    "sim_ts",
    
    # Utilities
    "hrf_smoothing_kernel",
    "shift",
    "soft_threshold",
    
    # I/O
    "write_results",
    
    # AFNI
    "afni_lm",
    "gen_afni_lm", 
    "build_afni_stims",
    "afni_hrf",
]

pyfmrireg - Python package for fMRI regression analysis.

A comprehensive framework for analyzing fMRI data with flexible modeling options.
"""
