#' Bootstrap Methods for fMRI Linear Models
#'
#' Functions for bootstrap inference including confidence intervals
#' and hypothesis testing
#'
#' @keywords internal
#' @noRd
#' @importFrom progress progress_bar
NULL

#' Bootstrap confidence intervals for GLM
#'
#' @description
#' Computes bootstrap confidence intervals using either
#' residual or case resampling. Handles temporal dependencies
#' via block bootstrap.
#'
#' @param fit_result Result from solve_integrated_glm
#' @param X Design matrix
#' @param Y Response matrix
#' @param config fmri_lm_config object
#' @param contrasts List of contrast matrices
#' @param nboot Number of bootstrap iterations
#' @param block_size Block size for temporal data
#' @param confidence Confidence level (default 0.95)
#' @param method Bootstrap method ("residual", "case", "wild")
#' @param parallel Use parallel processing
#' @param run_indices Run structure for multi-run data
#'
#' @return List with bootstrap distributions and confidence intervals
#' @keywords internal
#' @noRd
bootstrap_glm_inference <- function(fit_result, X, Y, config, 
                                   contrasts = NULL,
                                   nboot = 1000,
                                   block_size = NULL,
                                   confidence = 0.95,
                                   method = "residual",
                                   parallel = FALSE,
                                   run_indices = NULL) {
  
  n <- nrow(X)
  p <- ncol(X)
  nvoxels <- ncol(Y)
  
  # Determine block size if not provided
  if (is.null(block_size)) {
    block_size <- if (!is.null(run_indices)) {
      # Use run structure
      min(sapply(run_indices, length)) / 4
    } else {
      # Default based on sample size
      max(10, n / 20)
    }
    block_size <- round(block_size)
  }
  
  # Original estimates
  orig_betas <- fit_result$betas
  orig_contrasts <- if (!is.null(contrasts)) {
    lapply(contrasts, function(C) compute_contrast(fit_result, C))
  }
  
  # Bootstrap storage
  boot_betas <- array(NA, dim = c(nboot, p, nvoxels))
  boot_contrasts <- if (!is.null(contrasts)) {
    lapply(contrasts, function(C) {
      matrix(NA, nboot, nvoxels)
    })
  }
  
  # Fitted values and residuals
  fitted_vals <- X %*% orig_betas
  residuals <- Y - fitted_vals
  
  # Create blocks
  blocks <- create_bootstrap_blocks(n, block_size, run_indices)
  
  # Bootstrap iterations
  pb <- progress::progress_bar$new(total = nboot)
  
  for (b in 1:nboot) {
    pb$tick()
    
    # Generate bootstrap sample
    boot_data <- switch(method,
      "residual" = bootstrap_residual(X, fitted_vals, residuals, blocks),
      "case" = bootstrap_case(X, Y, blocks),
      "wild" = bootstrap_wild(X, fitted_vals, residuals),
      stop("Unknown bootstrap method: ", method)
    )
    
    # Fit bootstrap model
    boot_fit <- solve_integrated_glm(
      boot_data$X, 
      boot_data$Y, 
      config, 
      run_indices
    )
    
    # Store results
    boot_betas[b, , ] <- boot_fit$betas
    
    if (!is.null(contrasts)) {
      for (i in seq_along(contrasts)) {
        boot_con <- compute_contrast(boot_fit, contrasts[[i]])
        boot_contrasts[[i]][b, ] <- boot_con$estimate
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Compute confidence intervals
  alpha <- 1 - confidence
  probs <- c(alpha/2, 1 - alpha/2)
  
  # Beta CIs
  beta_ci <- apply(boot_betas, c(2, 3), quantile, probs = probs, na.rm = TRUE)
  beta_se_boot <- apply(boot_betas, c(2, 3), sd, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Contrast CIs
  contrast_ci <- if (!is.null(contrasts)) {
    lapply(boot_contrasts, function(bc) {
      t(apply(bc, 2, quantile, probs = probs, na.rm = TRUE))
    })
  }
  
  list(
    boot_betas = boot_betas,
    boot_contrasts = boot_contrasts,
    beta_ci = beta_ci,
    beta_se_boot = beta_se_boot,
    contrast_ci = contrast_ci,
    nboot = nboot,
    method = method,
    block_size = block_size,
    confidence = confidence
  )
}

#' Create bootstrap blocks
#'
#' @keywords internal
#' @noRd
create_bootstrap_blocks <- function(n, block_size, run_indices = NULL) {
  if (!is.null(run_indices)) {
    # Respect run structure
    blocks <- list()
    for (run in run_indices) {
      run_blocks <- split(
        run,
        ceiling(seq_along(run) / block_size)
      )
      blocks <- c(blocks, run_blocks)
    }
  } else {
    # Single run
    blocks <- split(
      1:n,
      ceiling(seq_along(1:n) / block_size)
    )
  }
  blocks
}

#' Residual bootstrap
#'
#' @keywords internal
#' @noRd
bootstrap_residual <- function(X, fitted, residuals, blocks) {
  n <- nrow(X)
  
  # Resample blocks of residuals
  sampled_blocks <- sample(length(blocks), replace = TRUE)
  resampled_idx <- unlist(blocks[sampled_blocks])
  
  # Adjust to exact size
  if (length(resampled_idx) > n) {
    resampled_idx <- resampled_idx[1:n]
  } else if (length(resampled_idx) < n) {
    extra <- sample(1:n, n - length(resampled_idx), replace = TRUE)
    resampled_idx <- c(resampled_idx, extra)
  }
  
  # New Y = fitted + resampled residuals
  Y_boot <- fitted + residuals[resampled_idx, , drop = FALSE]
  
  list(X = X, Y = Y_boot)
}

#' Case bootstrap
#'
#' @keywords internal
#' @noRd
bootstrap_case <- function(X, Y, blocks) {
  n <- nrow(X)
  
  # Resample blocks of cases
  sampled_blocks <- sample(length(blocks), replace = TRUE)
  resampled_idx <- unlist(blocks[sampled_blocks])
  
  # Adjust to exact size
  if (length(resampled_idx) > n) {
    resampled_idx <- resampled_idx[1:n]
  } else if (length(resampled_idx) < n) {
    extra <- sample(1:n, n - length(resampled_idx), replace = TRUE)
    resampled_idx <- c(resampled_idx, extra)
  }
  
  list(
    X = X[resampled_idx, , drop = FALSE],
    Y = Y[resampled_idx, , drop = FALSE]
  )
}

#' Wild bootstrap
#'
#' @keywords internal
#' @noRd
bootstrap_wild <- function(X, fitted, residuals) {
  n <- nrow(X)
  
  # Rademacher weights
  weights <- sample(c(-1, 1), n, replace = TRUE)
  
  # New Y = fitted + weighted residuals
  Y_boot <- fitted + residuals * weights
  
  list(X = X, Y = Y_boot)
}

#' Bootstrap hypothesis test
#'
#' @description
#' Performs bootstrap hypothesis test for contrasts using
#' the percentile method.
#'
#' @param boot_result Result from bootstrap_glm_inference
#' @param contrast_idx Index of contrast to test
#' @param null_value Null hypothesis value (default 0)
#'
#' @return P-value for two-sided test
#' @keywords internal
#' @noRd
bootstrap_hypothesis_test <- function(boot_result, contrast_idx, null_value = 0) {
  if (is.null(boot_result$boot_contrasts)) {
    stop("No contrasts in bootstrap results")
  }
  
  boot_dist <- boot_result$boot_contrasts[[contrast_idx]]
  
  # Compute p-value for each voxel
  nvoxels <- ncol(boot_dist)
  pvals <- numeric(nvoxels)
  
  for (v in 1:nvoxels) {
    # Two-sided test
    boot_centered <- boot_dist[, v] - mean(boot_dist[, v], na.rm = TRUE)
    observed <- mean(boot_dist[, v], na.rm = TRUE) - null_value
    
    pvals[v] <- mean(abs(boot_centered) >= abs(observed), na.rm = TRUE)
  }
  
  pvals
}

#' BCa confidence intervals
#'
#' @description
#' Computes bias-corrected and accelerated (BCa) bootstrap
#' confidence intervals for more accurate coverage.
#'
#' @param boot_dist Bootstrap distribution (nboot x nvoxels)
#' @param observed Observed estimates
#' @param confidence Confidence level
#'
#' @return Matrix of confidence intervals
#' @keywords internal
#' @noRd
compute_bca_ci <- function(boot_dist, observed, confidence = 0.95) {
  nboot <- nrow(boot_dist)
  nvoxels <- ncol(boot_dist)
  alpha <- 1 - confidence
  
  ci <- matrix(NA, 2, nvoxels)
  
  for (v in 1:nvoxels) {
    boot_v <- boot_dist[, v]
    obs_v <- observed[v]
    
    # Bias correction
    z0 <- qnorm(mean(boot_v <= obs_v, na.rm = TRUE))
    
    # Acceleration (would need jackknife for full implementation)
    a <- 0  # Simplified version
    
    # Adjusted percentiles
    z_alpha <- qnorm(c(alpha/2, 1 - alpha/2))
    p_adj <- pnorm(z0 + (z0 + z_alpha) / (1 - a * (z0 + z_alpha)))
    
    ci[, v] <- quantile(boot_v, probs = p_adj, na.rm = TRUE)
  }
  
  ci
}

file.content:
# Test bootstrap functionality

library(fmrireg)
library(testthat)

test_that("multiresponse_bootstrap_lm works with basic inputs", {
  # Create simple test data
  n <- 100
  p <- 3
  X <- cbind(1, matrix(rnorm(n * (p-1)), n, p-1))
  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * 5), n, 5)  # 5 response variables
  
  # Create data environment
  data_env <- list(.y = Y)
  
  # Create proper contrast objects
  con1 <- list(
    term = NULL,
    name = "effect1",
    weights = c(0, 1, 0),
    condnames = NULL,
    contrast_spec = NULL
  )
  class(con1) <- c("contrast", "list")
  attr(con1, "colind") <- 1:3  # Applies to all 3 columns of design matrix
  
  con2 <- list(
    term = NULL,
    name = "effect2", 
    weights = c(0, 0, 1),
    condnames = NULL,
    contrast_spec = NULL
  )
  class(con2) <- c("contrast", "list")
  attr(con2, "colind") <- 1:3  # Applies to all 3 columns of design matrix
  
  conlist <- list(con1 = con1, con2 = con2)
  
  # Run bootstrap
  result <- fmrireg:::multiresponse_bootstrap_lm(
    modmat = X,
    data_env = data_env,
    conlist = conlist,
    vnames = c("intercept", "x1", "x2"),
    fcon = NULL,
    nboot = 10,  # Small number for testing
    block_size = 20,
    event_indices = 2:3
  )
  
  expect_true(result$bootstrap)
  expect_equal(result$nboot, 10)
  expect_true(!is.null(result$con_cov))
  expect_true(!is.null(result$beta_cov))
  expect_equal(length(result$beta_cov), 2)  # Length should match event_indices length
})

test_that("multiresponse_bootstrap_lm validates inputs correctly", {
  X <- matrix(rnorm(50), 10, 5)
  Y <- matrix(rnorm(30), 10, 3)
  data_env <- list(.y = Y)
  
  # Test missing inputs
  expect_error(
    fmrireg:::multiresponse_bootstrap_lm(data_env = data_env, conlist = list()),
    "Either 'form' or 'modmat' must be provided"
  )
  
  # Test invalid block size
  expect_error(
    fmrireg:::multiresponse_bootstrap_lm(modmat = X, data_env = data_env, block_size = -1),
    "block_size must be a positive integer"
  )
  
  # Test invalid nboot
  expect_error(
    fmrireg:::multiresponse_bootstrap_lm(modmat = X, data_env = data_env, nboot = 0),
    "nboot must be a positive integer"
  )
  
  # Test dimension mismatch
  Y_wrong <- matrix(rnorm(20), 5, 4)  # Wrong number of rows
  expect_error(
    fmrireg:::multiresponse_bootstrap_lm(modmat = X, data_env = list(.y = Y_wrong)),
    "Number of rows in modmat"
  )
})

test_that("multiresponse_bootstrap_lm handles edge cases", {
  # Small dataset
  n <- 20
  p <- 2
  X <- cbind(1, rnorm(n))
  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * 2), n, 2)
  
  # Block size larger than data
  result <- fmrireg:::multiresponse_bootstrap_lm(
    modmat = X,
    data_env = list(.y = Y),
    conlist = list(),
    vnames = c("intercept", "x1"),
    fcon = NULL,
    nboot = 5,
    block_size = 30,  # Larger than n
    event_indices = 2
  )
  
  expect_true(result$bootstrap)
  
  # Single response
  Y_single <- matrix(rnorm(n), n, 1)
  result_single <- fmrireg:::multiresponse_bootstrap_lm(
    modmat = X,
    data_env = list(.y = Y_single),
    conlist = list(),
    vnames = c("intercept", "x1"),
    fcon = NULL,
    nboot = 5,
    block_size = 10,
    event_indices = 2
  )
  
  expect_equal(length(result_single$beta_cov), 1)  # Should have 1 element for event_indices = 2
})

test_that("bootstrap results are stable with set seed", {
  # Create data once
  n <- 50
  X <- cbind(1, rnorm(n))
  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * 3), n, 3)
  
  # Create proper contrast object
  con <- list(
    term = NULL,
    name = "slope",
    weights = c(0, 1),
    condnames = NULL,
    contrast_spec = NULL
  )
  class(con) <- c("contrast", "list")
  attr(con, "colind") <- 1:2
  conlist <- list(con = con)
  
  set.seed(123)
  result1 <- fmrireg:::multiresponse_bootstrap_lm(
    modmat = X,
    data_env = list(.y = Y),
    conlist = conlist,
    vnames = c("intercept", "x"),
    fcon = NULL,
    nboot = 20,
    block_size = 10,
    event_indices = 2
  )
  
  set.seed(123)
  result2 <- fmrireg:::multiresponse_bootstrap_lm(
    modmat = X,
    data_env = list(.y = Y),
    conlist = conlist,
    vnames = c("intercept", "x"),
    fcon = NULL,
    nboot = 20,
    block_size = 10,
    event_indices = 2
  )
  
  # Results should be identical with same seed
  expect_equal(result1$con_cov, result2$con_cov)
  expect_equal(result1$beta_cov, result2$beta_cov)
})

input.content:
"""
Bootstrap resampling methods for fMRI data.

Ported from fmrireg R package bootstrap functionality.
Provides residual, case, and wild bootstrap methods with block structure
to handle temporal dependencies in fMRI time series.
"""

import numpy as np
from typing import List, Dict, Optional, Union, Tuple


def create_bootstrap_blocks(n: int, 
                           block_size: int, 
                           run_indices: Optional[List[List[int]]] = None) -> List[List[int]]:
    """
    Create bootstrap blocks for resampling.
    
    Ported from fmrireg R function: create_bootstrap_blocks()
    Source: fmrireg/R/fmri_lm_bootstrap.R:144-163
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    n : int
        Total number of observations
    block_size : int
        Size of each block
    run_indices : list of lists, optional
        Run structure for multi-run data. Each inner list contains indices 
        for one run
        
    Returns
    -------
    list of lists
        Each inner list contains indices for one block
        
    Notes
    -----
    If run_indices is provided, blocks respect run boundaries to avoid
    mixing observations from different runs.
    """
    if run_indices is not None:
        # Respect run structure (matches R lines 145-154)
        blocks = []
        for run in run_indices:
            # Create blocks within each run
            run_array = np.array(run)
            n_run = len(run_array)
            
            # Split run into blocks
            n_blocks = int(np.ceil(n_run / block_size))
            for i in range(n_blocks):
                start_idx = i * block_size
                end_idx = min((i + 1) * block_size, n_run)
                block = run_array[start_idx:end_idx].tolist()
                blocks.append(block)
    else:
        # Single run (matches R lines 155-162)
        indices = list(range(n))
        blocks = []
        n_blocks = int(np.ceil(n / block_size))
        
        for i in range(n_blocks):
            start_idx = i * block_size
            end_idx = min((i + 1) * block_size, n)
            block = indices[start_idx:end_idx]
            blocks.append(block)
    
    return blocks


def bootstrap_residual(X: np.ndarray,
                      fitted: np.ndarray,
                      residuals: np.ndarray,
                      blocks: List[List[int]]) -> Dict[str, np.ndarray]:
    """
    Perform residual bootstrap by resampling blocks of residuals.
    
    Ported from fmrireg R function: bootstrap_residual()
    Source: fmrireg/R/fmri_lm_bootstrap.R:169-188
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    X : np.ndarray
        Design matrix (n_obs × n_params)
    fitted : np.ndarray
        Fitted values (n_obs × n_voxels)
    residuals : np.ndarray
        Residuals (n_obs × n_voxels)
    blocks : list of lists
        Bootstrap blocks created by create_bootstrap_blocks()
        
    Returns
    -------
    dict
        Dictionary with keys 'X' and 'Y' containing bootstrap sample
        
    Notes
    -----
    This method assumes residuals are independent after accounting for
    the model structure. It preserves the design matrix X and creates
    new Y by adding resampled residuals to fitted values.
    """
    X = np.asarray(X)
    fitted = np.asarray(fitted)
    residuals = np.asarray(residuals)
    
    n = X.shape[0]
    
    # Ensure fitted and residuals are 2D
    if fitted.ndim == 1:
        fitted = fitted.reshape(-1, 1)
    if residuals.ndim == 1:
        residuals = residuals.reshape(-1, 1)
    
    # Resample blocks of residuals (matches R lines 173-174)
    sampled_blocks = np.random.choice(len(blocks), size=len(blocks), replace=True)
    resampled_idx = []
    for block_idx in sampled_blocks:
        resampled_idx.extend(blocks[block_idx])
    
    # Adjust to exact size (matches R lines 176-182)
    if len(resampled_idx) > n:
        resampled_idx = resampled_idx[:n]
    elif len(resampled_idx) < n:
        extra = np.random.choice(n, n - len(resampled_idx), replace=True)
        resampled_idx.extend(extra.tolist())
    
    # New Y = fitted + resampled residuals (matches R line 185)
    Y_boot = fitted + residuals[resampled_idx, :]
    
    return {'X': X, 'Y': Y_boot}


def bootstrap_case(X: np.ndarray,
                  Y: np.ndarray,
                  blocks: List[List[int]]) -> Dict[str, np.ndarray]:
    """
    Perform case bootstrap by resampling blocks of observations.
    
    Ported from fmrireg R function: bootstrap_case()
    Source: fmrireg/R/fmri_lm_bootstrap.R:194-213
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    X : np.ndarray
        Design matrix (n_obs × n_params)
    Y : np.ndarray
        Response matrix (n_obs × n_voxels)
    blocks : list of lists
        Bootstrap blocks created by create_bootstrap_blocks()
        
    Returns
    -------
    dict
        Dictionary with keys 'X' and 'Y' containing bootstrap sample
        
    Notes
    -----
    This method resamples entire observations (both X and Y), which
    is appropriate when the design matrix can vary or when making
    fewer assumptions about the error structure.
    """
    X = np.asarray(X)
    Y = np.asarray(Y)
    
    n = X.shape[0]
    
    # Ensure Y is 2D
    if Y.ndim == 1:
        Y = Y.reshape(-1, 1)
    
    # Resample blocks of cases (matches R lines 198-199)
    sampled_blocks = np.random.choice(len(blocks), size=len(blocks), replace=True)
    resampled_idx = []
    for block_idx in sampled_blocks:
        resampled_idx.extend(blocks[block_idx])
    
    # Adjust to exact size (matches R lines 201-207)
    if len(resampled_idx) > n:
        resampled_idx = resampled_idx[:n]
    elif len(resampled_idx) < n:
        extra = np.random.choice(n, n - len(resampled_idx), replace=True)
        resampled_idx.extend(extra.tolist())
    
    # Return resampled X and Y (matches R lines 209-212)
    return {
        'X': X[resampled_idx, :],
        'Y': Y[resampled_idx, :]
    }


def bootstrap_wild(X: np.ndarray,
                  fitted: np.ndarray,
                  residuals: np.ndarray) -> Dict[str, np.ndarray]:
    """
    Perform wild bootstrap using Rademacher weights.
    
    Ported from fmrireg R function: bootstrap_wild()
    Source: fmrireg/R/fmri_lm_bootstrap.R:219-229
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    X : np.ndarray
        Design matrix (n_obs × n_params)
    fitted : np.ndarray
        Fitted values (n_obs × n_voxels)
    residuals : np.ndarray
        Residuals (n_obs × n_voxels)
        
    Returns
    -------
    dict
        Dictionary with keys 'X' and 'Y' containing bootstrap sample
        
    Notes
    -----
    Wild bootstrap is useful for heteroscedastic errors. It multiplies
    residuals by random weights (+1 or -1) to preserve the error
    distribution while allowing for heteroscedasticity.
    """
    X = np.asarray(X)
    fitted = np.asarray(fitted)
    residuals = np.asarray(residuals)
    
    n = X.shape[0]
    
    # Ensure fitted and residuals are 2D
    if fitted.ndim == 1:
        fitted = fitted.reshape(-1, 1)
    if residuals.ndim == 1:
        residuals = residuals.reshape(-1, 1)
    
    # Rademacher weights (matches R line 223)
    weights = np.random.choice([-1, 1], size=n, replace=True)
    
    # New Y = fitted + weighted residuals (matches R line 226)
    Y_boot = fitted + residuals * weights[:, np.newaxis]
    
    return {'X': X, 'Y': Y_boot}


def determine_block_size(n: int,
                        run_indices: Optional[List[List[int]]] = None,
                        default_fraction: float = 0.05) -> int:
    """
    Determine appropriate block size for bootstrap resampling.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    n : int
        Total number of observations
    run_indices : list of lists, optional
        Run structure for multi-run data
    default_fraction : float, optional
        Default fraction of total observations for block size
        
    Returns
    -------
    int
        Recommended block size
        
    Notes
    -----
    Block size should be large enough to capture temporal dependencies
    but small enough to allow sufficient randomization. For fMRI data,
    typical values are 10-50 timepoints depending on TR and analysis.
    """
    if run_indices is not None:
        # Use run structure - base on shortest run
        run_lengths = [len(run) for run in run_indices]
        min_run_length = min(run_lengths)
        # Use fraction of shortest run, but at least 10
        block_size = max(10, int(min_run_length * default_fraction))
    else:
        # Single run - use fraction of total, between 10 and n/4
        block_size = max(10, min(n // 4, int(n * default_fraction)))
    
    return block_size


def validate_bootstrap_inputs(X: np.ndarray,
                             Y: np.ndarray,
                             method: str,
                             nboot: int,
                             block_size: int) -> bool:
    """
    Validate inputs for bootstrap methods.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    X : np.ndarray
        Design matrix
    Y : np.ndarray
        Response matrix
    method : str
        Bootstrap method ('residual', 'case', 'wild')
    nboot : int
        Number of bootstrap iterations
    block_size : int
        Block size for resampling
        
    Returns
    -------
    bool
        True if inputs are valid
        
    Raises
    ------
    ValueError
        If inputs are invalid
    """
    # Check arrays
    X = np.asarray(X)
    Y = np.asarray(Y)
    
    if X.ndim != 2:
        raise ValueError("X must be a 2D array")
    
    if Y.ndim not in [1, 2]:
        raise ValueError("Y must be a 1D or 2D array")
    
    if X.shape[0] != Y.shape[0]:
        raise ValueError("X and Y must have same number of observations")
    
    # Check method
    valid_methods = ['residual', 'case', 'wild']
    if method not in valid_methods:
        raise ValueError(f"method must be one of {valid_methods}")
    
    # Check bootstrap parameters
    if not isinstance(nboot, int) or nboot < 1:
        raise ValueError("nboot must be a positive integer")
    
    if not isinstance(block_size, int) or block_size < 1:
        raise ValueError("block_size must be a positive integer")
    
    if block_size > X.shape[0]:
        raise ValueError("block_size cannot be larger than number of observations")
    
    return True

    if residuals.ndim == 1:
        residuals = residuals.reshape(-1, 1)
    
    # Rademacher weights (matches R line 223)
    weights = np.random.choice([-1, 1], size=n, replace=True)
    
    # New Y = fitted + weighted residuals (matches R line 226)
    Y_boot = fitted + residuals * weights[:, np.newaxis]
    
    return {'X': X, 'Y': Y_boot}


def determine_block_size(n: int,
                        run_indices: Optional[List[List[int]]] = None,
                        default_fraction: float = 0.05) -> int:
    """
    Determine appropriate block size for bootstrap resampling.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    n : int
        Total number of observations
    run_indices : list of lists, optional
        Run structure for multi-run data
    default_fraction : float, optional
        Default fraction of total observations for block size
        
    Returns
    -------
    int
        Recommended block size
        
    Notes
    -----
    Block size should be large enough to capture temporal dependencies
    but small enough to allow sufficient randomization. For fMRI data,
    typical values are 10-50 timepoints depending on TR and analysis.
    """
    if run_indices is not None:
        # Use run structure - base on shortest run
        run_lengths = [len(run) for run in run_indices]
        min_run_length = min(run_lengths)
        # Use fraction of shortest run, but at least 10
        block_size = max(10, int(min_run_length * default_fraction))
    else:
        # Single run - use fraction of total, between 10 and n/4
        block_size = max(10, min(n // 4, int(n * default_fraction)))
    
    return block_size


def validate_bootstrap_inputs(X: np.ndarray,
                             Y: np.ndarray,
                             method: str,
                             nboot: int,
                             block_size: int) -> bool:
    """
    Validate inputs for bootstrap methods.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    X : np.ndarray
        Design matrix
    Y : np.ndarray
        Response matrix
    method : str
        Bootstrap method ('residual', 'case', 'wild')
    nboot : int
        Number of bootstrap iterations
    block_size : int
        Block size for resampling
        
    Returns
    -------
    bool
        True if inputs are valid
        
    Raises
    ------
    ValueError
        If inputs are invalid
    """
    # Check arrays
    X = np.asarray(X)
    Y = np.asarray(Y)
    
    if X.ndim != 2:
        raise ValueError("X must be a 2D array")
    
    if Y.ndim not in [1, 2]:
        raise ValueError("Y must be a 1D or 2D array")
    
    if X.shape[0] != Y.shape[0]:
        raise ValueError("X and Y must have same number of observations")
    
    # Check method
    valid_methods = ['residual', 'case', 'wild']
    if method not in valid_methods:
        raise ValueError(f"method must be one of {valid_methods}")
    
    # Check bootstrap parameters
    if not isinstance(nboot, int) or nboot < 1:
        raise ValueError("nboot must be a positive integer")
    
    if not isinstance(block_size, int) or block_size < 1:
        raise ValueError("block_size must be a positive integer")
    
    if block_size > X.shape[0]:
        raise ValueError("block_size cannot be larger than number of observations")
    
    return True

"""
Bootstrap linear model fitting for fMRI data.

Ported from fmrireg R package bootstrap functionality.
Main functions for multiresponse bootstrap and GLM inference.
"""

import numpy as np
from typing import List, Dict, Optional, Union, Any, Tuple
import warnings

from ..models.fmri_lm import solve_glm_core, fast_preproject, GlmContext
from ..contrasts.contrast import compute_contrast, fit_contrasts
from .bootstrap_methods import (
    create_bootstrap_blocks,
    bootstrap_residual,
    bootstrap_case,
    bootstrap_wild,
    determine_block_size,
    validate_bootstrap_inputs
)


def multiresponse_bootstrap_lm(modmat: np.ndarray,
                              data_env: Dict[str, Any],
                              conlist: Optional[List] = None,
                              vnames: Optional[List[str]] = None,
                              fcon: Optional[np.ndarray] = None,
                              block_size: int = 30,
                              boot_rows: bool = False,
                              nboot: int = 100,
                              event_indices: Optional[List[int]] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """
    Multiresponse bootstrap linear model.
    
    Ported from fmrireg R function: multiresponse_bootstrap_lm()
    Source: fmrireg/R/bootstrap.R:51-196
    
    Performs block bootstrap resampling for multiresponse linear models,
    particularly useful for fMRI time series data where temporal dependencies exist.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    modmat : np.ndarray
        Model matrix (design matrix) (n_obs × n_params)
    data_env : dict
        Dictionary containing response data. Must have '.y' key with response matrix
    conlist : list, optional
        List of contrast objects to compute for each bootstrap sample
    vnames : list of str, optional
        Variable names for the design matrix columns
    fcon : np.ndarray, optional
        Fixed contrasts matrix
    block_size : int, optional
        Size of blocks for bootstrap (default 30)
    boot_rows : bool, optional
        Whether to bootstrap rows (default False, not implemented)
    nboot : int, optional
        Number of bootstrap iterations (default 100)
    event_indices : list of int, optional
        Indices of events for computing beta covariances
        
    Returns
    -------
    dict
        Dictionary containing:
        - 'original': Fitted original model with contrasts
        - 'con_cov': Covariance matrices for contrasts 
        - 'beta_cov': Covariance matrices for beta estimates
        - 'nboot': Number of bootstrap iterations performed
        - 'bootstrap': True (indicating this is a bootstrap result)
        
    Notes
    -----
    The function performs:
    1. Fits the original linear model
    2. Implements block bootstrap resampling of residuals
    3. Reconstructs response variables using fitted values and resampled residuals
    4. Computes contrasts for each bootstrap sample
    
    Block bootstrap helps preserve temporal dependencies by resampling
    blocks of consecutive observations rather than individual observations.
    """
    # Input validation (matches R lines 60-83)
    if modmat is None:
        raise ValueError("modmat must be provided")
    
    modmat = np.asarray(modmat)
    if modmat.ndim != 2:
        raise ValueError("modmat must be a matrix")
    
    if '.y' not in data_env:
        raise ValueError("data_env['.y'] must contain response variables")
    
    Y = np.asarray(data_env['.y'])
    if Y.ndim == 1:
        Y = Y.reshape(-1, 1)
    
    if modmat.shape[0] != Y.shape[0]:
        raise ValueError("Number of rows in modmat must match number of rows in response variables")
    
    if not isinstance(block_size, int) or block_size < 1:
        raise ValueError("block_size must be a positive integer")
    
    if not isinstance(nboot, int) or nboot < 1:
        raise ValueError("nboot must be a positive integer")
    
    # Fit original model (matches R lines 85-90)
    # Use numpy's least squares directly
    try:
        betas_orig, residuals_orig, rank, s = np.linalg.lstsq(modmat, Y, rcond=None)
        fitted_orig = modmat @ betas_orig
    except np.linalg.LinAlgError:
        raise ValueError("Failed to fit original model - possibly singular design matrix")
    
    # Prepare for bootstrap (matches R lines 92-107)
    n, p = modmat.shape
    nvoxels = Y.shape[1]
    
    # Create blocks for resampling (matches R lines 101-107)
    blocks = create_bootstrap_blocks(n, block_size)
    
    # Perform bootstrap (matches R lines 109-134)
    boot_results = []
    
    for b in range(nboot):
        # Generate bootstrap sample (matches R lines 112-129)
        boot_data = bootstrap_residual(modmat, fitted_orig, residuals_orig.T, blocks)
        Y_boot = boot_data['Y']
        
        # Fit bootstrap model (matches R line 132)
        try:
            betas_boot, _, _, _ = np.linalg.lstsq(modmat, Y_boot, rcond=None)
        except np.linalg.LinAlgError:
            # Skip this bootstrap sample if singular
            continue
        
        # Store results for contrast/beta covariance computation
        boot_results.append({
            'betas': betas_boot,
            'contrasts': {}
        })
        
        # Compute contrasts if provided (matches R line 133)
        if conlist is not None:
            for con_name, contrast_obj in conlist.items():
                # Extract contrast weights
                if hasattr(contrast_obj, 'weights'):
                    weights = np.asarray(contrast_obj.weights)
                    if weights.ndim == 1:
                        weights = weights.reshape(1, -1)
                    
                    # Compute contrast estimate
                    con_estimate = weights @ betas_boot
                    boot_results[-1]['contrasts'][con_name] = con_estimate
    
    # Handle case where some bootstrap samples failed
    if len(boot_results) == 0:
        raise RuntimeError("All bootstrap samples failed - check design matrix")
    
    if len(boot_results) < nboot:
        warnings.warn(f"Only {len(boot_results)} of {nboot} bootstrap samples succeeded")
    
    # Compute covariance matrices (matches R lines 136-158)
    con_cov = None
    if conlist is not None and len(conlist) > 0:
        con_cov = {}
        for con_name in conlist.keys():
            # Collect estimates across bootstrap samples
            estimates = []
            for br in boot_results:
                if con_name in br['contrasts']:
                    estimates.append(br['contrasts'][con_name])
            
            if len(estimates) > 1:
                estimates_array = np.array(estimates)
                # Reshape to (nboot × nvoxels) if needed
                if estimates_array.ndim == 3:
                    estimates_array = estimates_array.reshape(len(estimates), -1)
                con_cov[con_name] = np.cov(estimates_array.T)
            else:
                con_cov[con_name] = None
    
    # Compute beta covariance matrices (matches R lines 160-184)
    beta_cov = []
    if event_indices is not None:
        for event_idx in event_indices:
            # Collect beta estimates for this event across bootstrap samples
            estimates = []
            for br in boot_results:
                # event_idx is 1-based in R, convert to 0-based
                param_idx = event_idx - 1 if event_idx > 0 else event_idx
                if param_idx < br['betas'].shape[0]:
                    estimates.append(br['betas'][param_idx, :])
            
            if len(estimates) > 1:
                estimates_array = np.array(estimates)
                beta_cov.append(np.cov(estimates_array.T))
            else:
                beta_cov.append(None)
    
    # Fit original model with contrasts for return (matches R lines 186-195)
    # Create a simplified result structure
    original_result = {
        'betas': betas_orig,
        'residuals': residuals_orig.T,
        'fitted': fitted_orig,
        'rank': rank
    }
    
    # Add contrast results if provided
    if conlist is not None:
        contrasts_result = {}
        for con_name, contrast_obj in conlist.items():
            if hasattr(contrast_obj, 'weights'):
                weights = np.asarray(contrast_obj.weights)
                if weights.ndim == 1:
                    weights = weights.reshape(1, -1)
                con_estimate = weights @ betas_orig
                contrasts_result[con_name] = {
                    'estimate': con_estimate,
                    'data': [{'estimate': con_estimate}]  # Match expected structure
                }
        original_result['contrasts'] = contrasts_result
    
    # Add beta statistics if event_indices provided
    if event_indices is not None:
        bstats = {
            'data': [{'estimate': [betas_orig]}]  # Match expected structure
        }
        original_result['bstats'] = bstats
    
    # Return final result (matches R return structure)
    result = {
        'original': original_result,
        'con_cov': con_cov,
        'beta_cov': beta_cov,
        'nboot': len(boot_results),
        'bootstrap': True
    }
    
    return result


def bootstrap_glm_inference(fit_result: Dict[str, Any],
                           X: np.ndarray,
                           Y: np.ndarray,
                           contrasts: Optional[List[np.ndarray]] = None,
                           nboot: int = 1000,
                           block_size: Optional[int] = None,
                           confidence: float = 0.95,
                           method: str = "residual",
                           parallel: bool = False,
                           run_indices: Optional[List[List[int]]] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """
    Bootstrap confidence intervals for GLM.
    
    Ported from fmrireg R function: bootstrap_glm_inference()
    Source: fmrireg/R/fmri_lm_bootstrap.R:33-138
    
    Computes bootstrap confidence intervals using residual, case, or wild bootstrap.
    Handles temporal dependencies via block bootstrap.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    fit_result : dict
        Result from solve_glm_core or similar fitting function
    X : np.ndarray
        Design matrix (n_obs × n_params)
    Y : np.ndarray
        Response matrix (n_obs × n_voxels)
    contrasts : list of np.ndarray, optional
        List of contrast matrices to evaluate
    nboot : int, optional
        Number of bootstrap iterations (default 1000)
    block_size : int, optional
        Block size for temporal data. If None, determined automatically
    confidence : float, optional
        Confidence level (default 0.95)
    method : str, optional
        Bootstrap method ('residual', 'case', 'wild'), default 'residual'
    parallel : bool, optional
        Use parallel processing (not implemented)
    run_indices : list of lists, optional
        Run structure for multi-run data
        
    Returns
    -------
    dict
        Dictionary containing:
        - 'boot_betas': Bootstrap distribution of coefficients
        - 'boot_contrasts': Bootstrap distribution of contrasts
        - 'beta_ci': Confidence intervals for coefficients
        - 'beta_se_boot': Bootstrap standard errors for coefficients
        - 'contrast_ci': Confidence intervals for contrasts
        - 'nboot': Number of bootstrap iterations
        - 'method': Bootstrap method used
        - 'block_size': Block size used
        - 'confidence': Confidence level
    """
    # Validate inputs
    validate_bootstrap_inputs(X, Y, method, nboot, block_size or 10)
    
    X = np.asarray(X)
    Y = np.asarray(Y)
    if Y.ndim == 1:
        Y = Y.reshape(-1, 1)
    
    n, p = X.shape
    nvoxels = Y.shape[1]
    
    # Determine block size if not provided (matches R lines 46-56)
    if block_size is None:
        block_size = determine_block_size(n, run_indices)
    
    # Original estimates (matches R lines 58-62)
    orig_betas = fit_result['betas']
    orig_contrasts = None
    if contrasts is not None:
        orig_contrasts = []
        for C in contrasts:
            C = np.asarray(C)
            if C.ndim == 1:
                C = C.reshape(1, -1)
            orig_contrasts.append(C @ orig_betas)
    
    # Bootstrap storage (matches R lines 64-70)
    boot_betas = np.full((nboot, p, nvoxels), np.nan)
    boot_contrasts = None
    if contrasts is not None:
        boot_contrasts = []
        for C in contrasts:
            boot_contrasts.append(np.full((nboot, nvoxels), np.nan))
    
    # Fitted values and residuals (matches R lines 72-74)
    fitted_vals = X @ orig_betas
    residuals = Y - fitted_vals
    
    # Create blocks (matches R line 77)
    blocks = create_bootstrap_blocks(n, block_size, run_indices)
    
    # Bootstrap iterations (matches R lines 82-110)
    successful_boots = 0
    
    for b in range(nboot):
        try:
            # Generate bootstrap sample (matches R lines 85-91)
            if method == "residual":
                boot_data = bootstrap_residual(X, fitted_vals, residuals, blocks)
            elif method == "case":
                boot_data = bootstrap_case(X, Y, blocks)
            elif method == "wild":
                boot_data = bootstrap_wild(X, fitted_vals, residuals)
            else:
                raise ValueError(f"Unknown bootstrap method: {method}")
            
            # Fit bootstrap model (matches R lines 93-99)
            X_boot = boot_data['X']
            Y_boot = boot_data['Y']
            
            betas_boot, _, _, _ = np.linalg.lstsq(X_boot, Y_boot, rcond=None)
            
            # Store results (matches R line 102)
            boot_betas[successful_boots, :, :] = betas_boot
            
            # Compute contrasts (matches R lines 104-109)
            if contrasts is not None:
                for i, C in enumerate(contrasts):
                    C = np.asarray(C)
                    if C.ndim == 1:
                        C = C.reshape(1, -1)
                    con_boot = C @ betas_boot
                    boot_contrasts[i][successful_boots, :] = con_boot.flatten()
            
            successful_boots += 1
            
        except (np.linalg.LinAlgError, ValueError):
            # Skip failed bootstrap samples
            continue
    
    # Trim arrays to successful boots
    if successful_boots < nboot:
        warnings.warn(f"Only {successful_boots} of {nboot} bootstrap samples succeeded")
        boot_betas = boot_betas[:successful_boots, :, :]
        if boot_contrasts is not None:
            boot_contrasts = [bc[:successful_boots, :] for bc in boot_contrasts]
    
    # Compute confidence intervals (matches R lines 112-125)
    alpha = 1 - confidence
    probs = [alpha/2, 1 - alpha/2]
    
    # Beta CIs (matches R lines 117-118)
    beta_ci = np.quantile(boot_betas, probs, axis=0)
    beta_se_boot = np.std(boot_betas, axis=0)
    
    # Contrast CIs (matches R lines 120-125)
    contrast_ci = None
    if boot_contrasts is not None:
        contrast_ci = []
        for bc in boot_contrasts:
            ci = np.quantile(bc, probs, axis=0)
            contrast_ci.append(ci)
    
    return {
        'boot_betas': boot_betas,
        'boot_contrasts': boot_contrasts,
        'beta_ci': beta_ci,
        'beta_se_boot': beta_se_boot,
        'contrast_ci': contrast_ci,
        'nboot': successful_boots,
        'method': method,
        'block_size': block_size,
        'confidence': confidence
    }

            'data': [{'estimate': [betas_orig]}]  # Match expected structure
        }
        original_result['bstats'] = bstats
    
    # Return final result (matches R return structure)
    result = {
        'original': original_result,
        'con_cov': con_cov,
        'beta_cov': beta_cov,
        'nboot': len(boot_results),
        'bootstrap': True
    }
    
    return result


def bootstrap_glm_inference(fit_result: Dict[str, Any],
                           X: np.ndarray,
                           Y: np.ndarray,
                           contrasts: Optional[List[np.ndarray]] = None,
                           nboot: int = 1000,
                           block_size: Optional[int] = None,
                           confidence: float = 0.95,
                           method: str = "residual",
                           parallel: bool = False,
                           run_indices: Optional[List[List[int]]] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """
    Bootstrap confidence intervals for GLM.
    
    Ported from fmrireg R function: bootstrap_glm_inference()
    Source: fmrireg/R/fmri_lm_bootstrap.R:33-138
    
    Computes bootstrap confidence intervals using residual, case, or wild bootstrap.
    Handles temporal dependencies via block bootstrap.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    fit_result : dict
        Result from solve_glm_core or similar fitting function
    X : np.ndarray
        Design matrix (n_obs × n_params)
    Y : np.ndarray
        Response matrix (n_obs × n_voxels)
    contrasts : list of np.ndarray, optional
        List of contrast matrices to evaluate
    nboot : int, optional
        Number of bootstrap iterations (default 1000)
    block_size : int, optional
        Block size for temporal data. If None, determined automatically
    confidence : float, optional
        Confidence level (default 0.95)
    method : str, optional
        Bootstrap method ('residual', 'case', 'wild'), default 'residual'
    parallel : bool, optional
        Use parallel processing (not implemented)
    run_indices : list of lists, optional
        Run structure for multi-run data
        
    Returns
    -------
    dict
        Dictionary containing:
        - 'boot_betas': Bootstrap distribution of coefficients
        - 'boot_contrasts': Bootstrap distribution of contrasts
        - 'beta_ci': Confidence intervals for coefficients
        - 'beta_se_boot': Bootstrap standard errors for coefficients
        - 'contrast_ci': Confidence intervals for contrasts
        - 'nboot': Number of bootstrap iterations
        - 'method': Bootstrap method used
        - 'block_size': Block size used
        - 'confidence': Confidence level
    """
    # Validate inputs
    validate_bootstrap_inputs(X, Y, method, nboot, block_size or 10)
    
    X = np.asarray(X)
    Y = np.asarray(Y)
    if Y.ndim == 1:
        Y = Y.reshape(-1, 1)
    
    n, p = X.shape
    nvoxels = Y.shape[1]
    
    # Determine block size if not provided (matches R lines 46-56)
    if block_size is None:
        block_size = determine_block_size(n, run_indices)
    
    # Original estimates (matches R lines 58-62)
    orig_betas = fit_result['betas']
    orig_contrasts = None
    if contrasts is not None:
        orig_contrasts = []
        for C in contrasts:
            C = np.asarray(C)
            if C.ndim == 1:
                C = C.reshape(1, -1)
            orig_contrasts.append(C @ orig_betas)
    
    # Bootstrap storage (matches R lines 64-70)
    boot_betas = np.full((nboot, p, nvoxels), np.nan)
    boot_contrasts = None
    if contrasts is not None:
        boot_contrasts = []
        for C in contrasts:
            boot_contrasts.append(np.full((nboot, nvoxels), np.nan))
    
    # Fitted values and residuals (matches R lines 72-74)
    fitted_vals = X @ orig_betas
    residuals = Y - fitted_vals
    
    # Create blocks (matches R line 77)
    blocks = create_bootstrap_blocks(n, block_size, run_indices)
    
    # Bootstrap iterations (matches R lines 82-110)
    successful_boots = 0
    
    for b in range(nboot):
        try:
            # Generate bootstrap sample (matches R lines 85-91)
            if method == "residual":
                boot_data = bootstrap_residual(X, fitted_vals, residuals, blocks)
            elif method == "case":
                boot_data = bootstrap_case(X, Y, blocks)
            elif method == "wild":
                boot_data = bootstrap_wild(X, fitted_vals, residuals)
            else:
                raise ValueError(f"Unknown bootstrap method: {method}")
            
            # Fit bootstrap model (matches R lines 93-99)
            X_boot = boot_data['X']
            Y_boot = boot_data['Y']
            
            betas_boot, _, _, _ = np.linalg.lstsq(X_boot, Y_boot, rcond=None)
            
            # Store results (matches R line 102)
            boot_betas[successful_boots, :, :] = betas_boot
            
            # Compute contrasts (matches R lines 104-109)
            if contrasts is not None:
                for i, C in enumerate(contrasts):
                    C = np.asarray(C)
                    if C.ndim == 1:
                        C = C.reshape(1, -1)
                    con_boot = C @ betas_boot
                    boot_contrasts[i][successful_boots, :] = con_boot.flatten()
            
            successful_boots += 1
            
        except (np.linalg.LinAlgError, ValueError):
            # Skip failed bootstrap samples
            continue
    
    # Trim arrays to successful boots
    if successful_boots < nboot:
        warnings.warn(f"Only {successful_boots} of {nboot} bootstrap samples succeeded")
        boot_betas = boot_betas[:successful_boots, :, :]
        if boot_contrasts is not None:
            boot_contrasts = [bc[:successful_boots, :] for bc in boot_contrasts]
    
    # Compute confidence intervals (matches R lines 112-125)
    alpha = 1 - confidence
    probs = [alpha/2, 1 - alpha/2]
    
    # Beta CIs (matches R lines 117-118)
    beta_ci = np.quantile(boot_betas, probs, axis=0)
    beta_se_boot = np.std(boot_betas, axis=0)
    
    # Contrast CIs (matches R lines 120-125)
    contrast_ci = None
    if boot_contrasts is not None:
        contrast_ci = []
        for bc in boot_contrasts:
            ci = np.quantile(bc, probs, axis=0)
            contrast_ci.append(ci)
    
    return {
        'boot_betas': boot_betas,
        'boot_contrasts': boot_contrasts,
        'beta_ci': beta_ci,
        'beta_se_boot': beta_se_boot,
        'contrast_ci': contrast_ci,
        'nboot': successful_boots,
        'method': method,
        'block_size': block_size,
        'confidence': confidence
    }

"""
Bootstrap inference methods including hypothesis testing and confidence intervals.

Ported from fmrireg R package bootstrap inference functionality.
Provides hypothesis testing and advanced confidence interval methods.
"""

import numpy as np
from typing import List, Dict, Optional, Union, Tuple
from scipy import stats


def bootstrap_hypothesis_test(boot_result: Dict[str, any],
                             contrast_idx: int,
                             null_value: float = 0.0) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    Perform bootstrap hypothesis test for contrasts using percentile method.
    
    Ported from fmrireg R function: bootstrap_hypothesis_test()
    Source: fmrireg/R/fmri_lm_bootstrap.R:244-264
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    boot_result : dict
        Result from bootstrap_glm_inference containing bootstrap distributions
    contrast_idx : int
        Index of contrast to test (0-based)
    null_value : float, optional
        Null hypothesis value (default 0.0)
        
    Returns
    -------
    np.ndarray
        P-values for two-sided test for each voxel
        
    Notes
    -----
    Computes p-value using the bootstrap distribution by comparing
    the observed effect to the null hypothesis value.
    """
    if boot_result.get('boot_contrasts') is None:
        raise ValueError("No contrasts in bootstrap results")
    
    if contrast_idx >= len(boot_result['boot_contrasts']):
        raise ValueError(f"contrast_idx {contrast_idx} out of range")
    
    # Get bootstrap distribution for specified contrast (matches R line 249)
    boot_dist = boot_result['boot_contrasts'][contrast_idx]
    
    # Compute p-value for each voxel (matches R lines 252-261)
    nvoxels = boot_dist.shape[1]
    pvals = np.zeros(nvoxels)
    
    for v in range(nvoxels):
        # Two-sided test (matches R lines 257-260)
        boot_v = boot_dist[:, v]
        boot_v = boot_v[~np.isnan(boot_v)]  # Remove NaN values
        
        if len(boot_v) == 0:
            pvals[v] = np.nan
            continue
        
        # Center bootstrap distribution around zero
        boot_centered = boot_v - np.mean(boot_v)
        observed = np.mean(boot_v) - null_value
        
        # P-value as proportion of centered bootstrap exceeding observed difference
        pvals[v] = np.mean(np.abs(boot_centered) >= np.abs(observed))
    
    return pvals


def compute_bca_ci(boot_dist: np.ndarray,
                  observed: np.ndarray,
                  confidence: float = 0.95) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    Compute bias-corrected and accelerated (BCa) bootstrap confidence intervals.
    
    Ported from fmrireg R function: compute_bca_ci()
    Source: fmrireg/R/fmri_lm_bootstrap.R:279-304
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    boot_dist : np.ndarray
        Bootstrap distribution (nboot × nvoxels)
    observed : np.ndarray
        Observed estimates (nvoxels,)
    confidence : float, optional
        Confidence level (default 0.95)
        
    Returns
    -------
    np.ndarray
        Confidence intervals (2 × nvoxels), first row is lower bound,
        second row is upper bound
        
    Notes
    -----
    BCa intervals provide better coverage than percentile intervals
    by correcting for bias and skewness in the bootstrap distribution.
    This is a simplified version without full jackknife computation
    for the acceleration parameter.
    """
    boot_dist = np.asarray(boot_dist)
    observed = np.asarray(observed)
    
    if boot_dist.ndim != 2:
        raise ValueError("boot_dist must be 2D (nboot × nvoxels)")
    
    nboot, nvoxels = boot_dist.shape
    
    if len(observed) != nvoxels:
        raise ValueError("observed must have same length as number of voxels")
    
    alpha = 1 - confidence
    ci = np.full((2, nvoxels), np.nan)
    
    # Normal quantiles for confidence level
    z_alpha = stats.norm.ppf([alpha/2, 1 - alpha/2])
    
    for v in range(nvoxels):
        boot_v = boot_dist[:, v]
        obs_v = observed[v]
        
        # Remove NaN values
        valid_boots = ~np.isnan(boot_v)
        if np.sum(valid_boots) < 10:  # Need sufficient bootstrap samples
            continue
        
        boot_v = boot_v[valid_boots]
        
        # Bias correction (matches R line 291)
        # Proportion of bootstrap values <= observed
        prop_le = np.mean(boot_v <= obs_v)
        
        # Handle edge cases
        if prop_le == 0:
            prop_le = 1 / (2 * len(boot_v))
        elif prop_le == 1:
            prop_le = 1 - 1 / (2 * len(boot_v))
        
        z0 = stats.norm.ppf(prop_le)
        
        # Acceleration parameter (simplified version, matches R line 294)
        # Full implementation would require jackknife, here we use 0
        a = 0.0
        
        # Adjusted percentiles (matches R lines 297-298)
        z_adj = z0 + (z0 + z_alpha) / (1 - a * (z0 + z_alpha))
        
        # Convert back to probabilities
        p_adj = stats.norm.cdf(z_adj)
        
        # Ensure probabilities are in valid range
        p_adj = np.clip(p_adj, 0, 1)
        
        # Compute quantiles (matches R line 300)
        ci[:, v] = np.quantile(boot_v, p_adj)
    
    return ci


def bootstrap_confidence_intervals(boot_dist: np.ndarray,
                                  confidence: float = 0.95,
                                  method: str = 'percentile') -> np.ndarray:
    """
    Compute bootstrap confidence intervals using various methods.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    boot_dist : np.ndarray
        Bootstrap distribution (nboot × nvoxels)
    confidence : float, optional
        Confidence level (default 0.95)
    method : str, optional
        Method for computing CI ('percentile', 'basic', 'studentized')
        
    Returns
    -------
    np.ndarray
        Confidence intervals (2 × nvoxels)
        
    Notes
    -----
    Provides several bootstrap confidence interval methods:
    - percentile: Simple percentile method
    - basic: Basic bootstrap method (not implemented)
    - studentized: Studentized bootstrap (not implemented)
    """
    boot_dist = np.asarray(boot_dist)
    
    if boot_dist.ndim != 2:
        raise ValueError("boot_dist must be 2D (nboot × nvoxels)")
    
    alpha = 1 - confidence
    probs = [alpha/2, 1 - alpha/2]
    
    if method == 'percentile':
        # Simple percentile method
        ci = np.quantile(boot_dist, probs, axis=0)
        return ci
    
    elif method == 'basic':
        # Basic bootstrap method (Hall, 1988)
        # CI = 2*theta_hat - theta_boot_quantiles
        raise NotImplementedError("Basic bootstrap method not yet implemented")
    
    elif method == 'studentized':
        # Studentized bootstrap (requires bootstrap of bootstrap)
        raise NotImplementedError("Studentized bootstrap method not yet implemented")
    
    else:
        raise ValueError(f"Unknown method: {method}")


def compute_bootstrap_pvalues(boot_dist: np.ndarray,
                             observed: np.ndarray,
                             null_value: Union[float, np.ndarray] = 0.0,
                             alternative: str = 'two-sided') -> np.ndarray:
    """
    Compute bootstrap p-values for hypothesis testing.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    boot_dist : np.ndarray
        Bootstrap distribution (nboot × nvoxels)
    observed : np.ndarray
        Observed test statistics (nvoxels,)
    null_value : float or np.ndarray, optional
        Null hypothesis value(s) (default 0.0)
    alternative : str, optional
        Alternative hypothesis ('two-sided', 'greater', 'less')
        
    Returns
    -------
    np.ndarray
        P-values for each voxel
    """
    boot_dist = np.asarray(boot_dist)
    observed = np.asarray(observed)
    
    if boot_dist.ndim != 2:
        raise ValueError("boot_dist must be 2D")
    
    nboot, nvoxels = boot_dist.shape
    
    if len(observed) != nvoxels:
        raise ValueError("observed must match number of voxels")
    
    # Handle null_value
    if np.isscalar(null_value):
        null_value = np.full(nvoxels, null_value)
    else:
        null_value = np.asarray(null_value)
        if len(null_value) != nvoxels:
            raise ValueError("null_value must be scalar or match number of voxels")
    
    pvals = np.zeros(nvoxels)
    
    for v in range(nvoxels):
        boot_v = boot_dist[:, v]
        obs_v = observed[v]
        null_v = null_value[v]
        
        # Remove NaN values
        valid_boots = ~np.isnan(boot_v)
        if np.sum(valid_boots) == 0:
            pvals[v] = np.nan
            continue
        
        boot_v = boot_v[valid_boots]
        
        # Center bootstrap distribution around null
        boot_centered = boot_v - np.mean(boot_v) + null_v
        test_stat = obs_v - null_v
        
        # Compute p-value based on alternative hypothesis
        if alternative == 'two-sided':
            pvals[v] = np.mean(np.abs(boot_centered - null_v) >= np.abs(test_stat))
        elif alternative == 'greater':
            pvals[v] = np.mean(boot_centered >= obs_v)
        elif alternative == 'less':
            pvals[v] = np.mean(boot_centered <= obs_v)
        else:
            raise ValueError(f"Unknown alternative: {alternative}")
    
    return pvals


def bootstrap_effect_size(boot_dist: np.ndarray,
                         pooled_std: Optional[np.ndarray] = None) -> Dict[str, np.ndarray]:
    """
    Compute bootstrap-based effect size estimates.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    boot_dist : np.ndarray
        Bootstrap distribution of effect estimates (nboot × nvoxels)
    pooled_std : np.ndarray, optional
        Pooled standard deviation for Cohen's d calculation
        
    Returns
    -------
    dict
        Dictionary containing:
        - 'mean': Bootstrap mean effect size
        - 'std': Bootstrap standard deviation
        - 'cohens_d': Cohen's d (if pooled_std provided)
        - 'ci_95': 95% confidence interval
    """
    boot_dist = np.asarray(boot_dist)
    
    if boot_dist.ndim != 2:
        raise ValueError("boot_dist must be 2D")
    
    # Basic statistics
    boot_mean = np.nanmean(boot_dist, axis=0)
    boot_std = np.nanstd(boot_dist, axis=0)
    
    # 95% confidence interval
    ci_95 = np.nanquantile(boot_dist, [0.025, 0.975], axis=0)
    
    result = {
        'mean': boot_mean,
        'std': boot_std,
        'ci_95': ci_95
    }
    
    # Cohen's d if pooled standard deviation provided
    if pooled_std is not None:
        pooled_std = np.asarray(pooled_std)
        # Avoid division by zero
        valid_std = pooled_std > 0
        cohens_d = np.full_like(boot_mean, np.nan)
        cohens_d[valid_std] = boot_mean[valid_std] / pooled_std[valid_std]
        result['cohens_d'] = cohens_d
    
    return result


def validate_bootstrap_inference_inputs(boot_dist: np.ndarray,
                                       observed: Optional[np.ndarray] = None,
                                       confidence: float = 0.95) -> bool:
    """
    Validate inputs for bootstrap inference functions.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    boot_dist : np.ndarray
        Bootstrap distribution
    observed : np.ndarray, optional
        Observed values
    confidence : float, optional
        Confidence level
        
    Returns
    -------
    bool
        True if inputs are valid
        
    Raises
    ------
    ValueError
        If inputs are invalid
    """
    boot_dist = np.asarray(boot_dist)
    
    if boot_dist.ndim != 2:
        raise ValueError("boot_dist must be 2D (nboot × nvoxels)")
    
    if boot_dist.shape[0] < 10:
        raise ValueError("Need at least 10 bootstrap samples")
    
    if observed is not None:
        observed = np.asarray(observed)
        if observed.ndim != 1:
            raise ValueError("observed must be 1D")
        if len(observed) != boot_dist.shape[1]:
            raise ValueError("observed must match number of voxels in boot_dist")
    
    if not 0 < confidence < 1:
        raise ValueError("confidence must be between 0 and 1")
    
    return True

    observed : np.ndarray
        Observed test statistics (nvoxels,)
    null_value : float or np.ndarray, optional
        Null hypothesis value(s) (default 0.0)
    alternative : str, optional
        Alternative hypothesis ('two-sided', 'greater', 'less')
        
    Returns
    -------
    np.ndarray
        P-values for each voxel
    """
    boot_dist = np.asarray(boot_dist)
    observed = np.asarray(observed)
    
    if boot_dist.ndim != 2:
        raise ValueError("boot_dist must be 2D")
    
    nboot, nvoxels = boot_dist.shape
    
    if len(observed) != nvoxels:
        raise ValueError("observed must match number of voxels")
    
    # Handle null_value
    if np.isscalar(null_value):
        null_value = np.full(nvoxels, null_value)
    else:
        null_value = np.asarray(null_value)
        if len(null_value) != nvoxels:
            raise ValueError("null_value must be scalar or match number of voxels")
    
    pvals = np.zeros(nvoxels)
    
    for v in range(nvoxels):
        boot_v = boot_dist[:, v]
        obs_v = observed[v]
        null_v = null_value[v]
        
        # Remove NaN values
        valid_boots = ~np.isnan(boot_v)
        if np.sum(valid_boots) == 0:
            pvals[v] = np.nan
            continue
        
        boot_v = boot_v[valid_boots]
        
        # Center bootstrap distribution around null
        boot_centered = boot_v - np.mean(boot_v) + null_v
        test_stat = obs_v - null_v
        
        # Compute p-value based on alternative hypothesis
        if alternative == 'two-sided':
            pvals[v] = np.mean(np.abs(boot_centered - null_v) >= np.abs(test_stat))
        elif alternative == 'greater':
            pvals[v] = np.mean(boot_centered >= obs_v)
        elif alternative == 'less':
            pvals[v] = np.mean(boot_centered <= obs_v)
        else:
            raise ValueError(f"Unknown alternative: {alternative}")
    
    return pvals


def bootstrap_effect_size(boot_dist: np.ndarray,
                         pooled_std: Optional[np.ndarray] = None) -> Dict[str, np.ndarray]:
    """
    Compute bootstrap-based effect size estimates.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    boot_dist : np.ndarray
        Bootstrap distribution of effect estimates (nboot × nvoxels)
    pooled_std : np.ndarray, optional
        Pooled standard deviation for Cohen's d calculation
        
    Returns
    -------
    dict
        Dictionary containing:
        - 'mean': Bootstrap mean effect size
        - 'std': Bootstrap standard deviation
        - 'cohens_d': Cohen's d (if pooled_std provided)
        - 'ci_95': 95% confidence interval
    """
    boot_dist = np.asarray(boot_dist)
    
    if boot_dist.ndim != 2:
        raise ValueError("boot_dist must be 2D")
    
    # Basic statistics
    boot_mean = np.nanmean(boot_dist, axis=0)
    boot_std = np.nanstd(boot_dist, axis=0)
    
    # 95% confidence interval
    ci_95 = np.nanquantile(boot_dist, [0.025, 0.975], axis=0)
    
    result = {
        'mean': boot_mean,
        'std': boot_std,
        'ci_95': ci_95
    }
    
    # Cohen's d if pooled standard deviation provided
    if pooled_std is not None:
        pooled_std = np.asarray(pooled_std)
        # Avoid division by zero
        valid_std = pooled_std > 0
        cohens_d = np.full_like(boot_mean, np.nan)
        cohens_d[valid_std] = boot_mean[valid_std] / pooled_std[valid_std]
        result['cohens_d'] = cohens_d
    
    return result


def validate_bootstrap_inference_inputs(boot_dist: np.ndarray,
                                       observed: Optional[np.ndarray] = None,
                                       confidence: float = 0.95) -> bool:
    """
    Validate inputs for bootstrap inference functions.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    boot_dist : np.ndarray
        Bootstrap distribution
    observed : np.ndarray, optional
        Observed values
    confidence : float, optional
        Confidence level
        
    Returns
    -------
    bool
        True if inputs are valid
        
    Raises
    ------
    ValueError
        If inputs are invalid
    """
    boot_dist = np.asarray(boot_dist)
    
    if boot_dist.ndim != 2:
        raise ValueError("boot_dist must be 2D (nboot × nvoxels)")
    
    if boot_dist.shape[0] < 10:
        raise ValueError("Need at least 10 bootstrap samples")
    
    if observed is not None:
        observed = np.asarray(observed)
        if observed.ndim != 1:
            raise ValueError("observed must be 1D")
        if len(observed) != boot_dist.shape[1]:
            raise ValueError("observed must match number of voxels in boot_dist")
    
    if not 0 < confidence < 1:
        raise ValueError("confidence must be between 0 and 1")
    
    return True
